revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: 「'''横浜市登録地域文化財'''（よこはましとうろくちいきぶんかざい）は、[[横浜市]]が[http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki...」
revision.text:
'''横浜市登録地域文化財'''（よこはましとうろくちいきぶんかざい）は、[[横浜市]]が[http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001174.html#e000000737 横浜市文化財保護条例第6章（第45条～第49条）]の規定にしたがって施行する、一種の[[登録有形文化財]]保護制度。
横浜市内の各地域における歴史的[[文化遺産]]について、地域住民が守ってきたもの、地域を知る上で必要なものを選定し、その保護・活用を図るため、「横浜市登録地域文化財」(以下「地域文化財」）として登録することができる。

'''横浜市登録地域文化財'''（よこはましとうろくちいきぶんかざい）は、[http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001174.html#e000000737 横浜市文化財保護条例第6章（第45条～第49条）]の規定によって[[横浜市]]が独自に施行する、一種の[[登録有形文化財|登録文化財]]保護制度。
== 概要 == 横浜市内の各地域における歴史的[[文化遺産]]について、地域住民が守ってきたもの、地域を知る上で必要なものを選定し、その保護・活用を図るため、「横浜市登録地域文化財」(以下「地域文化財」）として登録することができる。
1996年（平成８年）の[[文化財保護法]]改正により、従来の[[指定文化財|指定有形文化財]]制度に加えて、文化財'''「[[登録有形文化財|登録]]」'''制度が創設された。有形民俗文化財、記念物（[[史跡]]・[[名勝]]・[[天然記念物]]関係）についても、登録制度が導入された。（文化財保護法の規定上、'''「指定」'''と'''「登録」'''とは明確に区別されているため、ウェブサイトや観光案内書等で「'''登録'''有形文化財として'''指定'''されている」などと表記するのは誤りである。）
本市地域文化財も、この登録制度に近いものであり、指定文化財制度を補完し、指定文化財制度ほどの法的強制力を与えず、地域住民の理解と協力により文化財を保護していくと言う'''「緩やかな保護」'''を目的とする制度である。
市内各区や地域の歴史愛好家や文化財の所有者からの要望を受け、各年度ごとに開かれる[http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kyoiku/shingikai/bunkazai/ 横浜市文化財保護審議会]の審議を経て登録される。
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001174.html 横浜市文化財保護条例] * [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001175.html 横浜市文化財保護条例施行規則] * [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kyoiku/shingikai/bunkazai/ 横浜市文化財保護審議会]
== 関連項目 == * [[文化財保護法]] * [[登録有形文化財]] * [[文化財]]

'''横浜市登録地域文化財'''（よこはましとうろくちいきぶんかざい）は、[http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001174.html#e000000737 横浜市文化財保護条例第6章（第45条～第49条）]の規定によって[[横浜市]]が独自に施行する、一種の[[登録有形文化財|登録文化財]]保護制度。
== 概要 == 横浜市内の各地域における歴史的[[文化遺産]]について、地域住民が守ってきたもの、地域を知る上で必要なものを選定し、その保護・活用を図るため、「横浜市登録地域文化財」(以下「地域文化財」）として登録することができる。
1996年（平成８年）の[[文化財保護法]]改正により、従来の[[指定文化財|指定有形文化財]]制度に加えて、文化財'''「[[登録有形文化財|登録]]」'''制度が創設された。有形民俗文化財、記念物（[[史跡]]・[[名勝]]・[[天然記念物]]関係）についても、登録制度が導入された。（文化財保護法の規定上、'''「指定」'''と'''「登録」'''とは明確に区別されているため、ウェブサイトや観光案内書等で「'''登録'''有形文化財として'''指定'''されている」などと表記するのは誤りである。）
本市地域文化財も、この登録制度に近いものであり、指定文化財制度を補完し、指定文化財制度ほどの法的強制力を与えず、地域住民の理解と協力により文化財を保護していくと言う'''「緩やかな保護」'''を目的とする制度である。
市内各区や地域の歴史愛好家や文化財の所有者からの要望を受け、各年度ごとに開かれる[http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kyoiku/shingikai/bunkazai/ 横浜市文化財保護審議会]の審議を経て登録される。

'''横浜市登録地域文化財'''（よこはましとうろくちいきぶんかざい）は、[http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001174.html#e000000737 横浜市文化財保護条例第6章（第45条～第49条）]の規定によって[[横浜市]]が独自に施行する、一種の[[登録有形文化財|登録文化財]]保護制度。

本市地域文化財も、この登録制度に近いものであり、指定文化財制度を補完し、指定文化財制度ほどの[[法的拘束力]]を与えず、地域住民の理解と協力により文化財を保護していくと言う'''「緩やかな保護」'''を目的とする制度である。
市内各区や地域の歴史愛好家や文化財の所有者からの要望を受け、各年度ごとに開かれる[http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kyoiku/shingikai/bunkazai/ 横浜市文化財保護審議会]の審議を経て登録される。

'''横浜市登録地域文化財'''（よこはましとうろくちいきぶんかざい）は、[http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001174.html#e000000737 横浜市文化財保護条例第6章（第45条～第49条）]の規定によって[[横浜市]]が独自に施行する、一種の[[登録有形文化財|登録文化財]]保護制度。
== 概要 == 横浜市内の各地域における歴史的[[文化遺産]]について、地域住民が守ってきたもの、地域を知る上で必要なものを選定し、その保護・活用を図るため、横浜市登録地域文化財として登録することができる。「地域文化財」・「横浜市地域文化財」と呼ばれることもある。
（「[[横浜市指定文化財および地域文化財一覧]]」の項も参照）
1996年（平成8年）の[[文化財保護法]]改正により、従来の[[指定文化財|指定有形文化財]]制度に加えて、文化財'''「[[登録有形文化財|登録]]」'''制度が創設された。有形民俗文化財、記念物（[[史跡]]・[[名勝]]・[[天然記念物]]関係）についても、登録制度が導入された。（[[文化財保護法]]の規定上、'''「指定」'''と'''「登録」'''とは明確に区別されているため、ウェブサイトや観光案内書等で「'''登録'''有形文化財として'''指定'''されている」などと表記するのは誤りである。）
本市地域文化財も、この登録制度に近いものであり、指定文化財制度を補完し、指定文化財制度ほどの[[法的拘束力]]を与えず、地域住民の理解と協力により文化財を保護していくと言う'''「緩やかな保護」'''を目的とする制度である。
市内各区や地域の歴史愛好家や文化財の所有者からの要望を受け、各年度ごとに開かれる[http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kyoiku/shingikai/bunkazai/ 横浜市文化財保護審議会]の審議を経て登録される。
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001174.html 横浜市文化財保護条例] * [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001175.html 横浜市文化財保護条例施行規則] * [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kyoiku/shingikai/bunkazai/ 横浜市文化財保護審議会]
== 関連項目 == * [[文化財保護法]] * [[登録有形文化財]] * [[文化財]] * [[横浜市指定文化財および地域文化財一覧]] * [[埋蔵文化財]]

== 概要 == 横浜市内の各地域における歴史的[[文化遺産]]について、地域住民が守ってきたもの、地域を知る上で必要なものを選定し、その保護・活用を図るため、横浜市登録地域文化財として登録することができる。「地域文化財」・「横浜市地域文化財」と呼ばれることもある。
（「'''[[横浜市指定文化財および地域文化財一覧]]'''」の項も参照）
1996年（平成8年）の[[文化財保護法]]改正により、従来の[[指定文化財|指定有形文化財]]制度に加えて、文化財'''「[[登録有形文化財|登録]]」'''制度が創設された。有形[[民俗文化財]]、[[記念物]]（[[史跡]]・[[名勝]]・[[天然記念物]]）についても、登録制度が導入された。（[[文化財保護法]]の規定上、'''「指定」'''と'''「登録」'''とは明確に区別されているため、ウェブサイトや観光案内書等で「'''登録'''有形文化財として'''指定'''されている」などと表記するのは誤りである。）
本市地域文化財も、この登録制度に近いものであるが、創設時期は国の登録文化財制度よりも古い1988年（昭和63年）である。指定文化財制度を補完し、指定文化財制度ほどの[[法的拘束力]]を与えず、地域住民の理解と協力により文化財を保護していくと言う'''「緩やかな保護」'''を目的とする制度である。
市内各区や地域の歴史愛好家や文化財の所有者からの要望を受け、各年度ごとに開かれる[http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kyoiku/shingikai/bunkazai/ 横浜市文化財保護審議会]の審議を経て登録される。
==脚注== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[文化財保護法]] * [[登録有形文化財]] * [[文化財]] * [[横浜市指定文化財および地域文化財一覧]] * [[埋蔵文化財]] * [[川崎市地域文化財]] - [[神奈川県]][[川崎市]]にあるよく似た制度。 * [[千葉市地域文化財]] - [[千葉県]][[千葉市]]にあるよく似た制度。
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001174.html 横浜市文化財保護条例] * [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001175.html 横浜市文化財保護条例施行規則] * [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kyoiku/shingikai/bunkazai/ 横浜市文化財保護審議会]

revision.comment: Created page with "[[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230}} '''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群遺跡}} dating from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to the early {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈..."
revision.text:
[[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230}}
'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群遺跡}} dating from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to the early {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|ja|港南台}}, {{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|ja|港南区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Kashio River Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}} (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22}}</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|ja|神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校}} (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|ja|神奈川県立港南台高等学校}}). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with {{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|ja|栄区}}, making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群}}, and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓}} design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓}}. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|ja|玄室}} with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)}}, ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}}, under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24}}</ref>
== Published Works == * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校}} Geography and History Research Club, ''A Study on the Cave Tomb Clusters along the Kashio River in Totsuka Ward, Yokohama'' (March 20, 1964). * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * Kōnan History Publishing Committee, ''History of Kōnan'' (October 1, 1979).
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * {{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)}}, ''[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf Maibun Yokohama No. 31 (Kashio River Cave Tomb Cluster)]'' (March 20, 2015).
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群}} * {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓}} ({{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓}}) * {{ill|Kashio River Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} * {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群}} * {{ill|Miyanomae Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群}} * {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}} * {{ill|List of Yokohama City Designated and Registered Cultural Properties|ja|横浜市指定文化財および地域文化財一覧}}
== External Links == *{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://teishoin.net/blog/003984.html|title=Current State of the Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|website=Kameno's Digital Photo Log|date=2010-09-22|accessdate=2020-07-22}}

== Distribution and Notable Yokoguchi Tomb concentration) == ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:港南区]], Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichishiyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:栄区]], Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:宮ノ前横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaoka Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:ひこしヶ谷横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Cave Tombs|ja|柏谷横穴群|en}}[[ja:柏谷横穴群]] ({{ill|Kannami|en}}[[ja:函南町]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:北江間横穴群]] ({{ill|Izunokuni|en}}[[ja:伊豆の国市]], Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|Eura Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:江浦横穴群]] ({{ill|Numazu|en}}[[ja:沼津市]], Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Yokoguchi Kofun|en}}[[ja:法皇山横穴古墳]] ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en}}[[ja:加賀市]], [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:高井田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en}}[[ja:柏原市]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfukuji Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:安福寺横穴群]] (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:十王免横穴群]] ({{ill|Matsue|en}}[[ja:松江市]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:狐谷横穴墓群]] (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:上塩冶横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en}}[[ja:出雲市]], Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:高津久横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en}}[[ja:知夫村]], [[Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en}}[[ja:竹並遺跡]] ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en}}[[ja:行橋市]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:城山横穴群]] ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福智町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en}}[[ja:水町遺跡]] ({{ill|Nōgata|en}}[[ja:直方市]], Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Yokoguchi|en}}[[ja:古月横穴]] ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:鞍手町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫ナギノ横穴群]] ({{ill|Tamana|en}}[[ja:玉名市]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫穴観音横穴群]] (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:鍋田横穴群]] ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en}}[[ja:山鹿市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:大村横穴群]] ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en}}[[ja:人吉市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:四日市横穴群]] ({{ill|Usa|en}}[[ja:宇佐市]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasunogaike Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site

{{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}}[[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230}}
'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群遺跡}} dating from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to the early {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|ja|港南台}}, {{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|ja|港南区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Kashio River Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}} (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22}}</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|ja|神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校}} (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|ja|神奈川県立港南台高等学校}}). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with {{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|ja|栄区}}, making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群}}, and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓}} design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓}}. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|ja|玄室}} with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)}}, ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}}, under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24}}</ref>
== Published Works == * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校}} Geography and History Research Club, ''A Study on the Cave Tomb Clusters along the Kashio River in Totsuka Ward, Yokohama'' (March 20, 1964). * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * Kōnan History Publishing Committee, ''History of Kōnan'' (October 1, 1979).

The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:港南台遺跡群]], and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課]], ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.

{{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]][[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230]]
'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:横穴墓群遺跡]] dating from the {{ill|Kofun period|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:古墳時代]] to the early {{ill|Nara period|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:奈良時代]], located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:港南台]], {{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:港南区]], {{ill|Yokohama|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:横浜市]], {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:神奈川県]]. It is part of the {{ill|Kashio River Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:㹨川流域横穴墓群]] and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]] (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22]]</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校]] (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:神奈川県立港南台高等学校]]). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with {{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:栄区]], making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:港南台遺跡群]], and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課]], ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓]]. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:玄室]] with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)]], ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]], under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:横浜市教育委員会]]|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24]]</ref>
== Published Works == * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:鵠沼女子高等学校]] Geography and History Research Club, ''A Study on the Cave Tomb Clusters along the Kashio River in Totsuka Ward, Yokohama'' (March 20, 1964). * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課]], ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * Kōnan History Publishing Committee, ''History of Kōnan'' (October 1, 1979).

{{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]][[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230]]
'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:横穴墓群遺跡]] dating from the {{ill|Kofun period|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:古墳時代]] to the early {{ill|Nara period|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:奈良時代]], located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:港南台]], {{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:港南区]], {{ill|Yokohama|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:横浜市]], {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:神奈川県]]. It is part of the {{ill|Kashio River Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:㹨川流域横穴墓群]] and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]] (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22]]</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校]] (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:神奈川県立港南台高等学校]]). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with {{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:栄区]], making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:港南台遺跡群]], and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課]], ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓]]. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:玄室]] with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)]], ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]], under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:横浜市教育委員会]]|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24]]</ref>
== Published Works == * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:鵠沼女子高等学校]] Geography and History Research Club, ''A Study on the Cave Tomb Clusters along the Kashio River in Totsuka Ward, Yokohama'' (March 20, 1964). * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課]], ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * Kōnan History Publishing Committee, ''History of Kōnan'' (October 1, 1979).

This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:玄室]] with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)]], ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]], under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:横浜市教育委員会]]|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24]]</ref>

{{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]][[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230]]
'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:横穴墓群遺跡]] dating from the {{ill|Kofun period|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:古墳時代]] to the early {{ill|Nara period|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:奈良時代]], located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:港南台]], {{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:港南区]], {{ill|Yokohama|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:横浜市]], {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:神奈川県]]. It is part of the {{ill|Itachikawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:㹨川流域横穴墓群]] and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]] (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22]]</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校]] (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:神奈川県立港南台高等学校]]). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with {{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:栄区]], making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:港南台遺跡群]], and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課]], ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓]]. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:玄室]] with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)]], ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]], under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:横浜市教育委員会]]|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24]]</ref>

Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230]]
'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:横穴墓群遺跡]] dating from the {{ill|Kofun period|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:古墳時代]] to the early {{ill|Nara period|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:奈良時代]], located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:港南台]], {{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:港南区]], {{ill|Yokohama|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:横浜市]], {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:神奈川県]]. It is part of the {{ill|Itachikawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:㹨川流域横穴墓群]] and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]] (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22]]</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校]] (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:神奈川県立港南台高等学校]]). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with {{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:栄区]], making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:港南台遺跡群]], and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課]], ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓]]. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:玄室]] with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)]], ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]], under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:横浜市教育委員会]]|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24]]</ref>

=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en}}[[ja:竹並遺跡]] ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en}}[[ja:行橋市]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:城山横穴群]] ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福智町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en}}[[ja:水町遺跡]] ({{ill|Nōgata|en}}[[ja:直方市]], Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Yokoguchi|en}}[[ja:古月横穴]] ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:鞍手町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫ナギノ横穴群]] ({{ill|Tamana|en}}[[ja:玉名市]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫穴観音横穴群]] (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:鍋田横穴群]] ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en}}[[ja:山鹿市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:大村横穴群]] ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en}}[[ja:人吉市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:四日市横穴群]] ({{ill|Usa|en}}[[ja:宇佐市]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site

revision.text:
{{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]][[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230]]
'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:横穴墓群遺跡]] dating from the {{ill|Kofun period|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:古墳時代]] to the early {{ill|Nara period|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:奈良時代]], located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:港南台]], {{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:港南区]], {{ill|Yokohama|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:横浜市]], {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:神奈川県]]. It is part of the {{ill|Itachikawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:㹨川流域横穴墓群]] and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]] (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22]]</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校]] (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:神奈川県立港南台高等学校]]). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with {{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:栄区]], making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:港南台遺跡群]], and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課]], ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓]]. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:玄室]] with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)]], ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]], under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:横浜市教育委員会]]|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24]]</ref>
== Published Works == * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:鵠沼女子高等学校]] Geography and History Research Club, ''A Study on the Cave Tomb Clusters along the Kashio River in Totsuka Ward, Yokohama'' (March 20, 1964). * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課]], ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * Kōnan History Publishing Committee, ''History of Kōnan'' (October 1, 1979).
== References == {{Reflist]]
== Further Reading == * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課]], ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * {{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)]], ''[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf Maibun Yokohama No. 31 (Itachikawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs)]'' (March 20, 2015).
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:港南台遺跡群]] * {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] ({{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓]]) * {{ill|Itachikawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:㹨川流域横穴墓群]] * {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] * {{ill|Miyanomae Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:宮ノ前横穴墓群]] * * {{ill|List of Yokohama City Designated and Registered Cultural Properties|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:横浜市指定文化財および地域文化財一覧]]
== External Links == *{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://teishoin.net/blog/003984.html|title=Current State of the Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|website=Kameno's Digital Photo Log|date=2010-09-22|accessdate=2020-07-22]]

== Distribution and Notable Yokoguchi Tomb concentration) == ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:港南区]], Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|en}}[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:栄区]], Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:宮ノ前横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:ひこしヶ谷横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Cave Tombs|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:柏谷横穴群|en}}[[ja:柏谷横穴群]] ({{ill|Kannami|en}}[[ja:函南町]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:北江間横穴群]] ({{ill|Izunokuni|en}}[[ja:伊豆の国市]], Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|Eura Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:江浦横穴群]] ({{ill|Numazu|en}}[[ja:沼津市]], Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Cave Tombs||ja|法皇山横穴古墳|en}}[[ja:法皇山横穴古墳]] ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en}}[[ja:加賀市]], [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:高井田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en}}[[ja:柏原市]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfukuji Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:安福寺横穴群]] (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:十王免横穴群]] ({{ill|Matsue|en}}[[ja:松江市]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:狐谷横穴墓群]] (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:上塩冶横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en}}[[ja:出雲市]], Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:高津久横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en}}[[ja:知夫村]], [[Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en}}[[ja:竹並遺跡]] ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en}}[[ja:行橋市]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:城山横穴群]] ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福智町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en}}[[ja:水町遺跡]] ({{ill|Nōgata|en}}[[ja:直方市]], Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Yokoguchi|en}}[[ja:古月横穴]] ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:鞍手町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫ナギノ横穴群]] ({{ill|Tamana|en}}[[ja:玉名市]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫穴観音横穴群]] (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:鍋田横穴群]] ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en}}[[ja:山鹿市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:大村横穴群]] ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en}}[[ja:人吉市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:四日市横穴群]] ({{ill|Usa|en}}[[ja:宇佐市]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site

Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with {{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:栄区]], making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:港南台遺跡群]], and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課]], ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓]]. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:玄室]] with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)]], ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>

{{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]][[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230]]
'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:横穴墓群遺跡]] dating from the {{ill|Kofun period|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:古墳時代]] to the early {{ill|Nara period|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:奈良時代]], located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:港南台]], {{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:港南区]], {{ill|Yokohama|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:横浜市]], {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:神奈川県]]. It is part of the {{ill|Itachikawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:㹨川流域横穴墓群]] and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]] (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22]]</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校]] (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:神奈川県立港南台高等学校]]). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with {{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:栄区]], making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:港南台遺跡群]], and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課]], ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓]]. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:玄室]] with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)]], ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]], under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:横浜市教育委員会]]|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24]]</ref>
== Published Works == * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:鵠沼女子高等学校]] Geography and History Research Club, ''A Study on the Cave Tomb Clusters along the Kashio River in Totsuka Ward, Yokohama'' (March 20, 1964). * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課]], ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * Kōnan History Publishing Committee, ''History of Kōnan'' (October 1, 1979).

This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:玄室]] with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)]], ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]], under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:横浜市教育委員会]]|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24]]</ref>

{{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]][[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230]]
'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:横穴墓群遺跡]] dating from the {{ill|Kofun period|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:古墳時代]] to the early {{ill|Nara period|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:奈良時代]], located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:港南台]], {{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:港南区]], {{ill|Yokohama|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:横浜市]], {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:神奈川県]]. It is part of the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:㹨川流域横穴墓群]] and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]] (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22]]</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校]] (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:神奈川県立港南台高等学校]]). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with {{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:栄区]], making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:港南台遺跡群]], and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課]], ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓]]. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:玄室]] with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)]], ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]], under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:横浜市教育委員会]]|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24]]</ref>

Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230]]
'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:横穴墓群遺跡]] dating from the {{ill|Kofun period|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:古墳時代]] to the early {{ill|Nara period|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:奈良時代]], located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:港南台]], {{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:港南区]], {{ill|Yokohama|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:横浜市]], {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:神奈川県]]. It is part of the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:㹨川流域横穴墓群]] and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]] (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22]]</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校]] (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:神奈川県立港南台高等学校]]). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with {{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:栄区]], making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:港南台遺跡群]], and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課]], ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓]]. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:玄室]] with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)]], ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]], under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:横浜市教育委員会]]|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24]]</ref>

Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with {{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:栄区]], making them closely associated with the Sakae area. === Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en}}[[ja:竹並遺跡]] ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en}}[[ja:行橋市]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:城山横穴群]] ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福智町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en}}[[ja:水町遺跡]] ({{ill|Nōgata|en}}[[ja:直方市]], Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Yokoguchi|en}}[[ja:古月横穴]] ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:鞍手町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫ナギノ横穴群]] ({{ill|Tamana|en}}[[ja:玉名市]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫穴観音横穴群]] (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:鍋田横穴群]] ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en}}[[ja:山鹿市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:大村横穴群]] ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en}}[[ja:人吉市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:四日市横穴群]] ({{ill|Usa|en}}[[ja:宇佐市]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site

revision.comment: Moving from [[Category:New pages]] to [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]][[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230]]
'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:横穴墓群遺跡]] dating from the {{ill|Kofun period|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:古墳時代]] to the early {{ill|Nara period|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:奈良時代]], located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:港南台]], {{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:港南区]], {{ill|Yokohama|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:横浜市]], {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:神奈川県]]. It is part of the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:㹨川流域横穴墓群]] and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]] (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22]]</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校]] (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:神奈川県立港南台高等学校]]). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with {{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:栄区]], making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:港南台遺跡群]], and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課]], ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓]]. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:玄室]] with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)]], ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]], under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:横浜市教育委員会]]|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24]]</ref>
== Published Works == * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:鵠沼女子高等学校]] Geography and History Research Club, ''A Study on the Cave Tomb Clusters along the Kashio River in Totsuka Ward, Yokohama'' (March 20, 1964). * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課]], ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * Kōnan History Publishing Committee, ''History of Kōnan'' (October 1, 1979).
== References == {{Reflist]]
== Further Reading == * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課]], ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * {{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)]], ''[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf Maibun Yokohama No. 31 (Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群)]'' (March 20, 2015).
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:港南台遺跡群]] * {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] ({{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓]]) * {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:㹨川流域横穴墓群]] * {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] * {{ill|Miyanomae Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:宮ノ前横穴墓群]] * * {{ill|List of Yokohama City Designated and Registered Cultural Properties|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:横浜市指定文化財および地域文化財一覧]]
== External Links == *{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://teishoin.net/blog/003984.html|title=Current State of the Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|website=Kameno's Digital Photo Log|date=2010-09-22|accessdate=2020-07-22]]
{{Japanese Cultural Properties]] {{coord|35|22|09.1|N|139|34|21.2|E|region:JP-14|display=title]]

=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Cave Tombs|en}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:柏谷横穴群|en}}[[ja:柏谷横穴群]] ({{ill|Kannami|en}}[[ja:函南町]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:北江間横穴群]] ({{ill|Izunokuni|en}}[[ja:伊豆の国市]], Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|Eura Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:江浦横穴群]] ({{ill|Numazu|en}}[[ja:沼津市]], Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Cave Tombs||ja|法皇山横穴古墳|en}}[[ja:法皇山横穴古墳]] ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en}}[[ja:加賀市]], [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|en}}[[ja:高井田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en}}[[ja:柏原市]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfukuji Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:安福寺横穴群]] (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:十王免横穴群]] ({{ill|Matsue|en}}[[ja:松江市]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:狐谷横穴墓群]] (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:上塩冶横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en}}[[ja:出雲市]], Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:高津久横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en}}[[ja:知夫村]], [[Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en}}[[ja:竹並遺跡]] ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en}}[[ja:行橋市]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:城山横穴群]] ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福智町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en}}[[ja:水町遺跡]] ({{ill|Nōgata|en}}[[ja:直方市]], Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Yokoguchi|en}}[[ja:古月横穴]] ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:鞍手町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫ナギノ横穴群]] ({{ill|Tamana|en}}[[ja:玉名市]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫穴観音横穴群]] (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:鍋田横穴群]] ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en}}[[ja:山鹿市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:大村横穴群]] ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en}}[[ja:人吉市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:四日市横穴群]] ({{ill|Usa|en}}[[ja:宇佐市]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site

{{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]][[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230]]
'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:横穴墓群遺跡]] dating from the {{ill|Kofun period|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:古墳時代]] to the early {{ill|Nara period|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:奈良時代]], located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:港南台]], {{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:港南区]], {{ill|Yokohama|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:横浜市]], {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:神奈川県]]. It is part of the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:㹨川流域横穴墓群]] and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]] (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22]]</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校]] (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:神奈川県立港南台高等学校]]). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with {{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:栄区]], making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:港南台遺跡群]], and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課]], ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓]]. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:玄室]] with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)]], ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]], under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:横浜市教育委員会]]|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24]]</ref>

[[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Japanese burial practices]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[ja:横穴墓]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]] {{Kofun navbar]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:fixing the kk issue]] [[Category:Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster forks]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property]][[Category:qq]]

{{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]][[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230]]
'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:横穴墓群遺跡]] dating from the {{ill|Kofun period|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:古墳時代]] to the early {{ill|Nara period|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:奈良時代]], located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:港南台]], {{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:港南区]], {{ill|Yokohama|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:横浜市]], {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:神奈川県]]. It is part of the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:㹨川流域横穴墓群]] and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]] (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22]]</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校]] (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:神奈川県立港南台高等学校]]). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with {{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:栄区]], making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:港南台遺跡群]], and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課]], ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓]]. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:玄室]] with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)]], ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]], under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:横浜市教育委員会]]|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24]]</ref>

Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230]]
'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:横穴墓群遺跡]] dating from the {{ill|Kofun period|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:古墳時代]] to the early {{ill|Nara period|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:奈良時代]], located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:港南台]], {{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:港南区]], {{ill|Yokohama|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:横浜市]], {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:神奈川県]]. It is part of the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:㹨川流域横穴墓群]] and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]] (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22]]</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校]] (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:神奈川県立港南台高等学校]]). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with {{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:栄区]], making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:港南台遺跡群]], and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課]], ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓]]. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:玄室]] with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)]], ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]], under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:横浜市教育委員会]]|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24]]</ref>
== Published Works == * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:鵠沼女子高等学校]] Geography and History Research Club, ''A Study on the Cave Tomb Clusters along the Kashio River in Totsuka Ward, Yokohama'' (March 20, 1964). * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課]], ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * Kōnan History Publishing Committee, ''History of Kōnan'' (October 1, 1979).
