'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:横穴墓群遺跡]] dating from the {{ill|Kofun period|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:古墳時代]] to the early {{ill|Nara period|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:奈良時代]], located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:港南台]], {{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:港南区]], {{ill|Yokohama|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:横浜市]], {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:神奈川県]]. It is part of the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:㹨川流域横穴墓群]] and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]] (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22]]</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校]] (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:神奈川県立港南台高等学校]]). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with {{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:栄区]], making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:港南台遺跡群]], and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課]], ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:鎌倉型横穴墓]] design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓]]. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:玄室]] with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)]], ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]], under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:横浜市教育委員会]]|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24]]</ref>

=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en}}[[ja:竹並遺跡]] ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en}}[[ja:行橋市]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:城山横穴群]] ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福智町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en}}[[ja:水町遺跡]] ({{ill|Nōgata|en}}[[ja:直方市]], Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Yokoguchi|en}}[[ja:古月横穴]] ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:鞍手町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫ナギノ横穴群]] ({{ill|Tamana|en}}[[ja:玉名市]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫穴観音横穴群]] (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:鍋田横穴群]] ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en}}[[ja:山鹿市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:大村横穴群]] ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en}}[[ja:人吉市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:四日市横穴群]] ({{ill|Usa|en}}[[ja:宇佐市]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site

pages]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:new pages]][[en:Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property]][[Category:new pages]] '''横浜市登録地域文化財'''（よこはましとうろくちいきぶんかざい）は、[http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001174.html#e000000737 横浜市文化財保護条例第6章（第45条～第49条）]の規定によって[[横浜市]]が独自に施行する[[登録有形文化財|登録文化財]]保護制度の一つである<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher=[[横浜市教育委員会]]|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0027_20240105.pdf|accessdate=2024-10-22}}</ref>。
[[File:生麦事件碑 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|市登録地域文化財の一つ、生麦事件碑]]
== 概要 == 横浜市内の各地域における歴史的[[文化遺産]]について、地域住民が守ってきたもの、地域を知る上で必要なものを選定し、その保護・活用を図るため、横浜市登録地域文化財として登録することができる。「地域文化財」・「横浜市地域文化財」と呼ばれることもある。
（「'''[[横浜市指定文化財および地域文化財一覧]]'''」の項も参照）
1996年（平成8年）の[[文化財保護法]]改正により、従来の[[指定文化財|指定有形文化財]]制度に加えて、文化財'''「[[登録有形文化財|登録]]」'''制度が創設された。有形[[民俗文化財]]、[[記念物]]（[[史跡]]・[[名勝]]・[[天然記念物]]）についても、登録制度が導入された。（[[文化財保護法]]の規定上、'''「指定」'''と'''「登録」'''とは明確に区別されているため、ウェブサイトや観光案内書等で「'''登録'''有形文化財として'''指定'''されている」などと表記するのは誤りである。）
本市地域文化財も、この登録制度に近いものであるが、創設時期は国の登録文化財制度よりも古い1988年（昭和63年）である。指定文化財制度を補完し、指定文化財制度ほどの[[法的拘束力]]を与えず、地域住民の理解と協力により文化財を保護していくと言う'''「緩やかな保護」'''を目的とする制度である。
市内各区や地域の歴史愛好家や文化財の所有者からの要望を受け、各年度ごとに開かれる[http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kyoiku/shingikai/bunkazai/ 横浜市文化財保護審議会]の審議を経て登録される。

City Registered Cultural Property-qq]] [[en:Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property-qq]] '''横浜市登録地域文化財'''（よこはましとうろくちいきぶんかざい）は、[http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001174.html#e000000737 横浜市文化財保護条例第6章（第45条～第49条）]の規定によって[[横浜市]]が独自に施行する[[登録有形文化財|登録文化財]]保護制度の一つである<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher=[[横浜市教育委員会]]|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0027_20240105.pdf|accessdate=2024-10-22}}</ref>。
[[File:生麦事件碑 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|市登録地域文化財の一つ、生麦事件碑]]
== 概要 == 横浜市内の各地域における歴史的[[文化遺産]]について、地域住民が守ってきたもの、地域を知る上で必要なものを選定し、その保護・活用を図るため、横浜市登録地域文化財として登録することができる。「地域文化財」・「横浜市地域文化財」と呼ばれることもある。
（「'''[[横浜市指定文化財および地域文化財一覧]]'''」の項も参照）
1996年（平成8年）の[[文化財保護法]]改正により、従来の[[指定文化財|指定有形文化財]]制度に加えて、文化財'''「[[登録有形文化財|登録]]」'''制度が創設された。有形[[民俗文化財]]、[[記念物]]（[[史跡]]・[[名勝]]・[[天然記念物]]）についても、登録制度が導入された。（[[文化財保護法]]の規定上、'''「指定」'''と'''「登録」'''とは明確に区別されているため、ウェブサイトや観光案内書等で「'''登録'''有形文化財として'''指定'''されている」などと表記するのは誤りである。）
本市地域文化財も、この登録制度に近いものであるが、創設時期は国の登録文化財制度よりも古い1988年（昭和63年）である。指定文化財制度を補完し、指定文化財制度ほどの[[法的拘束力]]を与えず、地域住民の理解と協力により文化財を保護していくと言う'''「緩やかな保護」'''を目的とする制度である。
市内各区や地域の歴史愛好家や文化財の所有者からの要望を受け、各年度ごとに開かれる[http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kyoiku/shingikai/bunkazai/ 横浜市文化財保護審議会]の審議を経て登録される。
==脚注== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[文化財保護法]] * [[登録有形文化財]] * [[文化財]] * [[横浜市指定文化財および地域文化財一覧]] * [[埋蔵文化財]] * [[川崎市地域文化財]] - [[神奈川県]][[川崎市]]にあるよく似た制度。 * [[千葉市地域文化財]] - [[千葉県]][[千葉市]]にあるよく似た制度。
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001174.html 横浜市文化財保護条例] * [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001175.html 横浜市文化財保護条例施行規則] * [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kyoiku/shingikai/bunkazai/ 横浜市文化財保護審議会]

City Registered Cultural Property-qq]] [[en:Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property-qq]] '''横浜市登録地域文化財'''（よこはましとうろくちいきぶんかざい）は、[http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001174.html#e000000737 横浜市文化財保護条例第6章（第45条～第49条）]の規定によって[[横浜市]]が独自に施行する[[登録有形文化財|登録文化財]]保護制度の一つである<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher=[[横浜市教育委員会]]|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0027_20240105.pdf|accessdate=2024-10-22}}</ref>。
[[File:生麦事件碑 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|市登録地域文化財の一つ、生麦事件碑]]
== 概要 == 横浜市内の各地域における歴史的[[文化遺産]]について、地域住民が守ってきたもの、地域を知る上で必要なものを選定し、その保護・活用を図るため、横浜市登録地域文化財として登録することができる。「地域文化財」・「横浜市地域文化財」と呼ばれることもある。
（「'''[[横浜市指定文化財および地域文化財一覧]]'''」の項も参照）
1996年（平成8年）の[[文化財保護法]]改正により、従来の[[指定文化財|指定有形文化財]]制度に加えて、文化財'''「[[登録有形文化財|登録]]」'''制度が創設された。有形[[民俗文化財]]、[[記念物]]（[[史跡]]・[[名勝]]・[[天然記念物]]）についても、登録制度が導入された。（[[文化財保護法]]の規定上、'''「指定」'''と'''「登録」'''とは明確に区別されているため、ウェブサイトや観光案内書等で「'''登録'''有形文化財として'''指定'''されている」などと表記するのは誤りである。）
本市地域文化財も、この登録制度に近いものであるが、創設時期は国の登録文化財制度よりも古い1988年（昭和63年）である。指定文化財制度を補完し、指定文化財制度ほどの[[法的拘束力]]を与えず、地域住民の理解と協力により文化財を保護していくと言う'''「緩やかな保護」'''を目的とする制度である。
市内各区や地域の歴史愛好家や文化財の所有者からの要望を受け、各年度ごとに開かれる[http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kyoiku/shingikai/bunkazai/ 横浜市文化財保護審議会]の審議を経て登録される。

City Registered Cultural Property-qq]] [[en:Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property-qq]] '''横浜市登録地域文化財'''（よこはましとうろくちいきぶんかざい）は、[http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001174.html#e000000737 横浜市文化財保護条例第6章（第45条～第49条）]の規定によって[[横浜市]]が独自に施行する[[登録有形文化財|登録文化財]]保護制度の一つである<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher=[[横浜市教育委員会]]|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0027_20240105.pdf|accessdate=2024-10-22}}</ref>。
[[File:生麦事件碑 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|市登録地域文化財の一つ、生麦事件碑]]
== 概要 == 横浜市内の各地域における歴史的[[文化遺産]]について、地域住民が守ってきたもの、地域を知る上で必要なものを選定し、その保護・活用を図るため、横浜市登録地域文化財として登録することができる。「地域文化財」・「横浜市地域文化財」と呼ばれることもある。
（「'''[[横浜市指定文化財および地域文化財一覧]]'''」の項も参照）
1996年（平成8年）の[[文化財保護法]]改正により、従来の[[指定文化財|指定有形文化財]]制度に加えて、文化財'''「[[登録有形文化財|登録]]」'''制度が創設された。有形[[民俗文化財]]、[[記念物]]（[[史跡]]・[[名勝]]・[[天然記念物]]）についても、登録制度が導入された。（[[文化財保護法]]の規定上、'''「指定」'''と'''「登録」'''とは明確に区別されているため、ウェブサイトや観光案内書等で「'''登録'''有形文化財として'''指定'''されている」などと表記するのは誤りである。）
本市地域文化財も、この登録制度に近いものであるが、創設時期は国の登録文化財制度よりも古い1988年（昭和63年）である。指定文化財制度を補完し、指定文化財制度ほどの[[法的拘束力]]を与えず、地域住民の理解と協力により文化財を保護していくと言う'''「緩やかな保護」'''を目的とする制度である。
市内各区や地域の歴史愛好家や文化財の所有者からの要望を受け、各年度ごとに開かれる[http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kyoiku/shingikai/bunkazai/ 横浜市文化財保護審議会]の審議を経て登録される。

'''Yokohama City Registered Regional Cultural Property''' (よこはましとうろくちいきぶんかざい) is one of the protection systems for [[registered tangible cultural properties]] uniquely implemented by {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市}} under the provisions of the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001174.html#e000000737 {{ill|Yokohama City Cultural Property Protection Ordinance|ja|横浜市文化財保護条例}}] Chapter 6 (Articles 45–49).<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0027_20240105.pdf|accessdate=2024-10-22}}</ref>
[[File:生麦事件碑 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|One example of a City Registered Regional Cultural Property: the Namamugi Incident Monument]]
== Overview == This system allows for the registration of historic [[cultural heritage]] within various areas of Yokohama City—items that have been preserved by local residents and are essential for understanding the region—in order to promote their protection and utilization. Properties registered under this system are also referred to as "Regional Cultural Properties" or "Yokohama City Regional Cultural Properties."
(See also the entry on '''[[Yokohama City Designated Cultural Properties and Regional Cultural Properties List]]'''.)
Following the 1996 (Heisei 8) amendment to the {{ill|Cultural Property Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法}}, a registration system for cultural properties was established in addition to the conventional system for designated tangible cultural properties. This registration system was also extended to tangible [[folk cultural properties]] and [[memorials]] (including [[historical sites]], [[places of scenic beauty]], and [[natural monuments]]). (Under the provisions of the Cultural Property Protection Law, the terms '''"designation"''' and '''"registration"''' are clearly distinguished, so it is incorrect to state on websites or tourist guides that a property is "designated" when it is in fact "registered.")
The Yokohama City Regional Cultural Property system, while similar to the national registration system, was actually established earlier, in 1988 (Shōwa 63). It is designed to complement the designated cultural property system by providing a more "relaxed form of protection"—one that does not impose the same level of legal binding force, relying instead on the understanding and cooperation of local residents.
Properties are registered following reviews held annually by the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kyoiku/shingikai/bunkazai/ {{ill|Yokohama City Cultural Property Protection Council|ja|横浜市文化財保護審議会}}], which convene each fiscal year based on requests from local history enthusiasts and cultural property owners.

'''Yokohama City Registered Regional Cultural Property''' (よこはましとうろくちいきぶんかざい) is one of the protection systems for [[registered tangible cultural properties]] uniquely implemented by {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市}} under the provisions of the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001174.html#e000000737] Chapter 6 (Articles 45–49).<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0027_20240105.pdf|accessdate=2024-10-22}}</ref>
[[File:生麦事件碑 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|One example of a City Registered Regional Cultural Property: the Namamugi Incident Monument]]
== Overview == This system allows for the registration of historic [[cultural heritage]] within various areas of Yokohama City—items that have been preserved by local residents and are essential for understanding the region—in order to promote their protection and utilization. Properties registered under this system are also referred to as "Regional Cultural Properties" or "Yokohama City Regional Cultural Properties."
(See also the entry on '''[[Yokohama City Designated Cultural Properties and Regional Cultural Properties List]]'''.)
Following the 1996 (Heisei 8) amendment to the {{ill|Cultural Property Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法}}, a registration system for cultural properties was established in addition to the conventional system for designated tangible cultural properties. This registration system was also extended to tangible [[folk cultural properties]] and [[memorials]] (including [[historical sites]], [[places of scenic beauty]], and [[natural monuments]]). (Under the provisions of the Cultural Property Protection Law, the terms '''"designation"''' and '''"registration"''' are clearly distinguished, so it is incorrect to state on websites or tourist guides that a property is "designated" when it is in fact "registered.")
The Yokohama City Regional Cultural Property system, while similar to the national registration system, was actually established earlier, in 1988 (Shōwa 63). It is designed to complement the designated cultural property system by providing a more "relaxed form of protection"—one that does not impose the same level of legal binding force, relying instead on the understanding and cooperation of local residents.
Properties are registered following reviews held annually by the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kyoiku/shingikai/bunkazai/], which convene each fiscal year based on requests from local history enthusiasts and cultural property owners.

(Under the provisions of the Cultural Property Protection Law, the terms '''"designation"''' and '''"registration"''' are clearly distinguished, so it is incorrect to state on websites or tourist guides that a property is "designated" when it is in fact "registered.")
The Yokohama City Regional Cultural Property system, while similar to the national registration system, was actually established earlier, in 1988 (Shōwa 63). It is designed to complement the designated cultural property system by providing a more "relaxed form of protection"—one that does not impose the same level of legal binding force, relying instead on the understanding and cooperation of local residents.
Properties are registered following reviews held annually by the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kyoiku/shingikai/bunkazai/], which convene each fiscal year based on requests from local history enthusiasts and cultural property owners.

City Registered Cultural Property-qq]] [[en:Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property-qq]] {{出典の明記|date=2018-02-11}} '''Yokohama City Registered Regional Cultural Property''' (横浜市登録地域文化財) is one of the protection systems for [[registered tangible cultural properties]] uniquely implemented by {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市}} under the provisions of the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001174.html#e000000737] Chapter 6 (Articles 45–49).<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0027_20240105.pdf|accessdate=2024-10-22}}</ref>
[[File:生麦事件碑 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|One example of a City Registered Regional Cultural Property: the Namamugi Incident Monument]]
== Overview == This system allows for the registration of historic [[cultural heritage]] within various areas of Yokohama City—items that have been preserved by local residents and are essential for understanding the region—in order to promote their protection and utilization. Properties registered under this system are also referred to as "Regional Cultural Properties" or "Yokohama City Regional Cultural Properties."
(See also the entry on '''[[Yokohama City Designated Cultural Properties and Regional Cultural Properties List]]'''.)
Following the 1996 (Heisei 8) amendment to the {{ill|Cultural Property Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法}}, a registration system for cultural properties was established in addition to the conventional system for designated tangible cultural properties. This registration system was also extended to tangible [[folk cultural properties]] and [[memorials]] (including [[historical sites]], [[places of scenic beauty]], and [[natural monuments]]). (Under the provisions of the Cultural Property Protection Law, the terms '''"designation"''' and '''"registration"''' are clearly distinguished, so it is incorrect to state on websites or tourist guides that a property is "designated" when it is in fact "registered.")
The Yokohama City Regional Cultural Property system, while similar to the national registration system, was actually established earlier, in 1988 (Shōwa 63). It is designed to complement the designated cultural property system by providing a more "relaxed form of protection"—one that does not impose the same level of legal binding force, relying instead on the understanding and cooperation of local residents.
Properties are registered following reviews held annually by the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kyoiku/shingikai/bunkazai/], which convene each fiscal year based on requests from local history enthusiasts and cultural property owners.

pages]] [[Category:qq-Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property—>[[category:qq close]] {{出典の明記|date=2018-02-11}} '''Yokohama City Registered Regional Cultural Property''' (横浜市登録地域文化財) is one of the protection systems for [[registered tangible cultural properties]] uniquely implemented by {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市}} under the provisions of the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001174.html#e000000737] Chapter 6 (Articles 45–49).<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0027_20240105.pdf|accessdate=2024-10-22}}</ref>
[[File:生麦事件碑 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|One example of a City Registered Regional Cultural Property: the Namamugi Incident Monument]]
== Overview == This system allows for the registration of historic [[cultural heritage]] within various areas of Yokohama City—items that have been preserved by local residents and are essential for understanding the region—in order to promote their protection and utilization. Properties registered under this system are also referred to as "Regional Cultural Properties" or "Yokohama City Regional Cultural Properties."
(See also the entry on '''[[Yokohama City Designated Cultural Properties and Regional Cultural Properties List]]'''.)
Following the 1996 (Heisei 8) amendment to the {{ill|Cultural Property Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法}}, a registration system for cultural properties was established in addition to the conventional system for designated tangible cultural properties. This registration system was also extended to tangible [[folk cultural properties]] and [[memorials]] (including [[historical sites]], [[places of scenic beauty]], and [[natural monuments]]). (Under the provisions of the Cultural Property Protection Law, the terms '''"designation"''' and '''"registration"''' are clearly distinguished, so it is incorrect to state on websites or tourist guides that a property is "designated" when it is in fact "registered.")
The Yokohama City Regional Cultural Property system, while similar to the national registration system, was actually established earlier, in 1988 (Shōwa 63). It is designed to complement the designated cultural property system by providing a more "relaxed form of protection"—one that does not impose the same level of legal binding force, relying instead on the understanding and cooperation of local residents.
Properties are registered following reviews held annually by the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kyoiku/shingikai/bunkazai/], which convene each fiscal year based on requests from local history enthusiasts and cultural property owners.

Properties registered under this system are also referred to as "Regional Cultural Properties" or "Yokohama City Regional Cultural Properties."

The Yokohama City Regional Cultural Property system, while similar to the national registration system, was actually established earlier, in 1988 (Shōwa 63). open]]<!--Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property-->[[category:qq close]] {{出典の明記|date=2018-02-11}} '''Yokohama City Registered Regional Cultural Property''' (横浜市登録地域文化財) is one of the protection systems for [[registered tangible cultural properties]] uniquely implemented by {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市}} under the provisions of the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001174.html#e000000737] Chapter 6 (Articles 45–49).<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0027_20240105.pdf|accessdate=2024-10-22}}</ref>
[[File:生麦事件碑 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|One example of a City Registered Regional Cultural Property: the Namamugi Incident Monument]]
== Overview == This system allows for the registration of historic [[cultural heritage]] within various areas of Yokohama City—items that have been preserved by local residents and are essential for understanding the region—in order to promote their protection and utilization. Properties registered under this system are also referred to as "Regional Cultural Properties" or "Yokohama City Regional Cultural Properties."
(See also the entry on '''[[Yokohama City Designated Cultural Properties and Regional Cultural Properties List]]'''.)
Following the 1996 (Heisei 8) amendment to the {{ill|Cultural Property Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法}}, a registration system for cultural properties was established in addition to the conventional system for designated tangible cultural properties. This registration system was also extended to tangible [[folk cultural properties]] and [[memorials]] (including [[historical sites]], [[places of scenic beauty]], and [[natural monuments]]). (Under the provisions of the Cultural Property Protection Law, the terms '''"designation"''' and '''"registration"''' are clearly distinguished, so it is incorrect to state on websites or tourist guides that a property is "designated" when it is in fact "registered.")
The Yokohama City Regional Cultural Property system, while similar to the national registration system, was actually established earlier, in 1988 (Shōwa 63). It is designed to complement the designated cultural property system by providing a more "relaxed form of protection"—one that does not impose the same level of legal binding force, relying instead on the understanding and cooperation of local residents.
Properties are registered following reviews held annually by the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kyoiku/shingikai/bunkazai/], which convene each fiscal year based on requests from local history enthusiasts and cultural property owners.
== Footnotes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * [[Cultural Property Protection Law]] * [[Registered Tangible Cultural Property]] * [[Cultural Property]] * [[Yokohama City Designated Cultural Properties and Regional Cultural Properties List]] * [[Buried Cultural Properties]] * [[Kawasaki City Regional Cultural Properties]] – A similar system in [[Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Kawasaki City]]. * [[Chiba City Regional Cultural Properties]] – A similar system in [[Chiba Prefecture]] [[Chiba City]].
== External Links == * [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001174.html Yokohama City Cultural Property Protection Ordinance] * [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001175.html Yokohama City Cultural Property Protection Ordinance Enforcement Regulations] * [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kyoiku/shingikai/bunkazai/ Yokohama City Cultural Property Protection Council]

Registered Cultural Property}}[[Category:qq]] {{出典の明記|date=2018-02-11}} '''Yokohama City Registered Regional Cultural Property''' (横浜市登録地域文化財) is one of the protection systems for [[registered tangible cultural properties]] uniquely implemented by {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市}} under the provisions of the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001174.html#e000000737] Chapter 6 (Articles 45–49).<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0027_20240105.pdf|accessdate=2024-10-22}}</ref>
[[File:生麦事件碑 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|One example of a City Registered Regional Cultural Property: the Namamugi Incident Monument]]
== Overview == This system allows for the registration of historic [[cultural heritage]] within various areas of Yokohama City—items that have been preserved by local residents and are essential for understanding the region—in order to promote their protection and utilization. Properties registered under this system are also referred to as "Regional Cultural Properties" or "Yokohama City Regional Cultural Properties."
(See also the entry on '''[[Yokohama City Designated Cultural Properties and Regional Cultural Properties List]]'''.)
Following the 1996 (Heisei 8) amendment to the {{ill|Cultural Property Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法}}, a registration system for cultural properties was established in addition to the conventional system for designated tangible cultural properties. This registration system was also extended to tangible [[folk cultural properties]] and [[memorials]] (including [[historical sites]], [[places of scenic beauty]], and [[natural monuments]]). (Under the provisions of the Cultural Property Protection Law, the terms '''"designation"''' and '''"registration"''' are clearly distinguished, so it is incorrect to state on websites or tourist guides that a property is "designated" when it is in fact "registered.")
The Yokohama City Regional Cultural Property system, while similar to the national registration system, was actually established earlier, in 1988 (Shōwa 63). It is designed to complement the designated cultural property system by providing a more "relaxed form of protection"—one that does not impose the same level of legal binding force, relying instead on the understanding and cooperation of local residents.
Properties are registered following reviews held annually by the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kyoiku/shingikai/bunkazai/], which convene each fiscal year based on requests from local history enthusiasts and cultural property owners.

[[Category:Unsorted_completion_categories]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] <!--Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property-->[[Category:qq]][[sn:Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property}}[[Category:qq]] {{出典の明記|date=2018-02-11}} '''Yokohama City Registered Regional Cultural Property''' (横浜市登録地域文化財) is one of the protection systems for [[registered tangible cultural properties]] uniquely implemented by {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市}} under the provisions of the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001174.html#e000000737] Chapter 6 (Articles 45–49).<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0027_20240105.pdf|accessdate=2024-10-22}}</ref>
[[File:生麦事件碑 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|One example of a City Registered Regional Cultural Property: the Namamugi Incident Monument]]
== Overview == This system allows for the registration of historic [[cultural heritage]] within various areas of Yokohama City—items that have been preserved by local residents and are essential for understanding the region—in order to promote their protection and utilization. Properties registered under this system are also referred to as "Regional Cultural Properties" or "Yokohama City Regional Cultural Properties."
(See also the entry on '''[[Yokohama City Designated Cultural Properties and Regional Cultural Properties List]]'''.)
Following the 1996 (Heisei 8) amendment to the {{ill|Cultural Property Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法}}, a registration system for cultural properties was established in addition to the conventional system for designated tangible cultural properties. This registration system was also extended to tangible [[folk cultural properties]] and [[memorials]] (including [[historical sites]], [[places of scenic beauty]], and [[natural monuments]]). (Under the provisions of the Cultural Property Protection Law, the terms '''"designation"''' and '''"registration"''' are clearly distinguished, so it is incorrect to state on websites or tourist guides that a property is "designated" when it is in fact "registered.")
The Yokohama City Regional Cultural Property system, while similar to the national registration system, was actually established earlier, in 1988 (Shōwa 63). It is designed to complement the designated cultural property system by providing a more "relaxed form of protection"—one that does not impose the same level of legal binding force, relying instead on the understanding and cooperation of local residents.
Properties are registered following reviews held annually by the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kyoiku/shingikai/bunkazai/], which convene each fiscal year based on requests from local history enthusiasts and cultural property owners.

[[Category:Unsorted_completion_categories]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] <!--Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property-->[[Category:qq]][[sn:Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property}}[[Category:qq]] {{出典の明記|date=2018-02-11}} '''Yokohama City Registered Regional Cultural Property''' (横浜市登録地域文化財) is one of the protection systems for [[registered tangible cultural properties]] uniquely implemented by {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市}} under the provisions of the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001174.html#e000000737] Chapter 6 (Articles 45–49).<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0027_20240105.pdf|accessdate=2024-10-22}}</ref>
[[File:生麦事件碑 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|One example of a City Registered Regional Cultural Property: the Namamugi Incident Monument]]
== Overview == This system allows for the registration of historic [[cultural heritage]] within various areas of Yokohama City—items that have been preserved by local residents and are essential for understanding the region—in order to promote their protection and utilization. Properties registered under this system are also referred to as "Regional Cultural Properties" or "Yokohama City Regional Cultural Properties."
(See also the entry on '''[[Yokohama City Designated Cultural Properties and Regional Cultural Properties List]]'''.)
Following the 1996 (Heisei 8) amendment to the {{ill|Cultural Property Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法}}, a registration system for cultural properties was established in addition to the conventional system for designated tangible cultural properties. This registration system was also extended to tangible [[folk cultural properties]] and [[memorials]] (including [[historical sites]], [[places of scenic beauty]], and [[natural monuments]]). (Under the provisions of the Cultural Property Protection Law, the terms '''"designation"''' and '''"registration"''' are clearly distinguished, so it is incorrect to state on websites or tourist guides that a property is "designated" when it is in fact "registered.")
The Yokohama City Regional Cultural Property system, while similar to the national registration system, was actually established earlier, in 1988 (Shōwa 63). It is designed to complement the designated cultural property system by providing a more "relaxed form of protection"—one that does not impose the same level of legal binding force, relying instead on the understanding and cooperation of local residents.
Properties are registered following reviews held annually by the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kyoiku/shingikai/bunkazai/], which convene each fiscal year based on requests from local history enthusiasts and cultural property owners.

Property]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property}}[[Category:qq]] [[en:Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property]] [[Category:Unsorted_completion_categories]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] <!--Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property-->[[Category:qq]][[sn:Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property}}[[Category:qq]] {{出典の明記|date=2018-02-11}} '''Yokohama City Registered Regional Cultural Property''' (横浜市登録地域文化財) is one of the protection systems for [[registered tangible cultural properties]] uniquely implemented by {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市}} under the provisions of the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001174.html#e000000737] Chapter 6 (Articles 45–49).<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0027_20240105.pdf|accessdate=2024-10-22}}</ref>
[[File:生麦事件碑 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|One example of a City Registered Regional Cultural Property: the Namamugi Incident Monument]]
== Overview == This system allows for the registration of historic [[cultural heritage]] within various areas of Yokohama City—items that have been preserved by local residents and are essential for understanding the region—in order to promote their protection and utilization. Properties registered under this system are also referred to as "Regional Cultural Properties" or "Yokohama City Regional Cultural Properties."
(See also the entry on '''[[Yokohama City Designated Cultural Properties and Regional Cultural Properties List]]'''.)
Following the 1996 (Heisei 8) amendment to the {{ill|Cultural Property Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法}}, a registration system for cultural properties was established in addition to the conventional system for designated tangible cultural properties. This registration system was also extended to tangible [[folk cultural properties]] and [[memorials]] (including [[historical sites]], [[places of scenic beauty]], and [[natural monuments]]). (Under the provisions of the Cultural Property Protection Law, the terms '''"designation"''' and '''"registration"''' are clearly distinguished, so it is incorrect to state on websites or tourist guides that a property is "designated" when it is in fact "registered.")
The Yokohama City Regional Cultural Property system, while similar to the national registration system, was actually established earlier, in 1988 (Shōwa 63). It is designed to complement the designated cultural property system by providing a more "relaxed form of protection"—one that does not impose the same level of legal binding force, relying instead on the understanding and cooperation of local residents.
Properties are registered following reviews held annually by the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kyoiku/shingikai/bunkazai/], which convene each fiscal year based on requests from local history enthusiasts and cultural property owners.
== Footnotes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * [[Cultural Property Protection Law]] * [[Registered Tangible Cultural Property]] * [[Cultural Property]] * [[Yokohama City Designated Cultural Properties and Regional Cultural Properties List]] * [[Buried Cultural Properties]] * [[Kawasaki City Regional Cultural Properties]] – A similar system in [[Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Kawasaki City]]. * [[Chiba City Regional Cultural Properties]] – A similar system in [[Chiba Prefecture]] [[Chiba City]].
== External Links == * [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001174.html Yokohama City Cultural Property Protection Ordinance] * [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001175.html Yokohama City Cultural Property Protection Ordinance Enforcement Regulations] * [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kyoiku/shingikai/bunkazai/ Yokohama City Cultural Property Protection Council]

Property]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property}}[[Category:qq]] {{出典の明記|date=2018-02-11}} '''Yokohama City Registered Regional Cultural Property''' (横浜市登録地域文化財) is one of the protection systems for [[registered tangible cultural properties]] uniquely implemented by {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市}} under the provisions of the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001174.html#e000000737] Chapter 6 (Articles 45–49).<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0027_20240105.pdf|accessdate=2024-10-22}}</ref>
[[File:生麦事件碑 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|One example of a City Registered Regional Cultural Property: the Namamugi Incident Monument]]
== Overview == This system allows for the registration of historic [[cultural heritage]] within various areas of Yokohama City—items that have been preserved by local residents and are essential for understanding the region—in order to promote their protection and utilization. Properties registered under this system are also referred to as "Regional Cultural Properties" or "Yokohama City Regional Cultural Properties."
(See also the entry on '''[[Yokohama City Designated Cultural Properties and Regional Cultural Properties List]]'''.)
Following the 1996 (Heisei 8) amendment to the {{ill|Cultural Property Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法}}, a registration system for cultural properties was established in addition to the conventional system for designated tangible cultural properties. This registration system was also extended to tangible [[folk cultural properties]] and [[memorials]] (including [[historical sites]], [[places of scenic beauty]], and [[natural monuments]]). (Under the provisions of the Cultural Property Protection Law, the terms '''"designation"''' and '''"registration"''' are clearly distinguished, so it is incorrect to state on websites or tourist guides that a property is "designated" when it is in fact "registered.")
The Yokohama City Regional Cultural Property system, while similar to the national registration system, was actually established earlier, in 1988 (Shōwa 63). It is designed to complement the designated cultural property system by providing a more "relaxed form of protection"—one that does not impose the same level of legal binding force, relying instead on the understanding and cooperation of local residents.
Properties are registered following reviews held annually by the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kyoiku/shingikai/bunkazai/], which convene each fiscal year based on requests from local history enthusiasts and cultural property owners.

Property]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property}}[[Category:11]] {{出典の明記|date=2018-02-11}} '''Yokohama City Registered Regional Cultural Property''' (横浜市登録地域文化財) is one of the protection systems for [[registered tangible cultural properties]] uniquely implemented by {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市}} under the provisions of the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001174.html#e000000737] Chapter 6 (Articles 45–49).<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0027_20240105.pdf|accessdate=2024-10-22}}</ref>
[[File:生麦事件碑 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|One example of a City Registered Regional Cultural Property: the Namamugi Incident Monument]]
== Overview == This system allows for the registration of historic [[cultural heritage]] within various areas of Yokohama City—items that have been preserved by local residents and are essential for understanding the region—in order to promote their protection and utilization. Properties registered under this system are also referred to as "Regional Cultural Properties" or "Yokohama City Regional Cultural Properties."
(See also the entry on '''[[Yokohama City Designated Cultural Properties and Regional Cultural Properties List]]'''.)
Following the 1996 (Heisei 8) amendment to the {{ill|Cultural Property Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法}}, a registration system for cultural properties was established in addition to the conventional system for designated tangible cultural properties. This registration system was also extended to tangible [[folk cultural properties]] and [[memorials]] (including [[historical sites]], [[places of scenic beauty]], and [[natural monuments]]). (Under the provisions of the Cultural Property Protection Law, the terms '''"designation"''' and '''"registration"''' are clearly distinguished, so it is incorrect to state on websites or tourist guides that a property is "designated" when it is in fact "registered.")
The Yokohama City Regional Cultural Property system, while similar to the national registration system, was actually established earlier, in 1988 (Shōwa 63). It is designed to complement the designated cultural property system by providing a more "relaxed form of protection"—one that does not impose the same level of legal binding force, relying instead on the understanding and cooperation of local residents.
Properties are registered following reviews held annually by the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kyoiku/shingikai/bunkazai/], which convene each fiscal year based on requests from local history enthusiasts and cultural property owners.

Property]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property}}[[Category:11]] [[en:Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] <!--Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property-->[[Category:11]][[sn:Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property}}[[Category:11]] {{出典の明記|date=2018-02-11}} '''Yokohama City Registered Regional Cultural Property''' (横浜市登録地域文化財) is one of the protection systems for [[registered tangible cultural properties]] uniquely implemented by {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市}} under the provisions of the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001174.html#e000000737] Chapter 6 (Articles 45–49).<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0027_20240105.pdf|accessdate=2024-10-22}}</ref>
[[File:生麦事件碑 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|One example of a City Registered Regional Cultural Property: the Namamugi Incident Monument]]
== Overview == This system allows for the registration of historic [[cultural heritage]] within various areas of Yokohama City—items that have been preserved by local residents and are essential for understanding the region—in order to promote their protection and utilization. Properties registered under this system are also referred to as "Regional Cultural Properties" or "Yokohama City Regional Cultural Properties."
(See also the entry on '''[[Yokohama City Designated Cultural Properties and Regional Cultural Properties List]]'''.)
Following the 1996 (Heisei 8) amendment to the {{ill|Cultural Property Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法}}, a registration system for cultural properties was established in addition to the conventional system for designated tangible cultural properties. This registration system was also extended to tangible [[folk cultural properties]] and [[memorials]] (including [[historical sites]], [[places of scenic beauty]], and [[natural monuments]]). (Under the provisions of the Cultural Property Protection Law, the terms '''"designation"''' and '''"registration"''' are clearly distinguished, so it is incorrect to state on websites or tourist guides that a property is "designated" when it is in fact "registered.")
The Yokohama City Regional Cultural Property system, while similar to the national registration system, was actually established earlier, in 1988 (Shōwa 63). It is designed to complement the designated cultural property system by providing a more "relaxed form of protection"—one that does not impose the same level of legal binding force, relying instead on the understanding and cooperation of local residents.
Properties are registered following reviews held annually by the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kyoiku/shingikai/bunkazai/], which convene each fiscal year based on requests from local history enthusiasts and cultural property owners.

Property}}[[Category:11]] {{出典の明記|date=2018-02-11}} '''Yokohama City Registered Regional Cultural Property''' (横浜市登録地域文化財) is one of the protection systems for [[registered tangible cultural properties]] uniquely implemented by {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市}} under the provisions of the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001174.html#e000000737] Chapter 6 (Articles 45–49).<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0027_20240105.pdf|accessdate=2024-10-22}}</ref>
[[File:生麦事件碑 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|One example of a City Registered Regional Cultural Property: the Namamugi Incident Monument]]
== Overview == This system allows for the registration of historic [[cultural heritage]] within various areas of Yokohama City—items that have been preserved by local residents and are essential for understanding the region—in order to promote their protection and utilization. Properties registered under this system are also referred to as "Regional Cultural Properties" or "Yokohama City Regional Cultural Properties."
(See also the entry on '''[[Yokohama City Designated Cultural Properties and Regional Cultural Properties List]]'''.)
Following the 1996 (Heisei 8) amendment to the {{ill|Cultural Property Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法}}, a registration system for cultural properties was established in addition to the conventional system for designated tangible cultural properties. This registration system was also extended to tangible [[folk cultural properties]] and [[memorials]] (including [[historical sites]], [[places of scenic beauty]], and [[natural monuments]]). (Under the provisions of the Cultural Property Protection Law, the terms '''"designation"''' and '''"registration"''' are clearly distinguished, so it is incorrect to state on websites or tourist guides that a property is "designated" when it is in fact "registered.")
The Yokohama City Regional Cultural Property system, while similar to the national registration system, was actually established earlier, in 1988 (Shōwa 63). It is designed to complement the designated cultural property system by providing a more "relaxed form of protection"—one that does not impose the same level of legal binding force, relying instead on the understanding and cooperation of local residents.
Properties are registered following reviews held annually by the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kyoiku/shingikai/bunkazai/], which convene each fiscal year based on requests from local history enthusiasts and cultural property owners.

Property}}[[Category:11]] {{出典の明記|date=2018-02-11}} '''Yokohama City Registered Regional Cultural Property''' (横浜市登録地域文化財) is one of the protection systems for [[registered tangible cultural properties]] uniquely implemented by {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市}} under the provisions of the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001174.html#e000000737] Chapter 6 (Articles 45–49).<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0027_20240105.pdf|accessdate=2024-10-22}}</ref>
[[File:生麦事件碑 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|One example of a City Registered Regional Cultural Property: the Namamugi Incident Monument]]
== Overview == This system allows for the registration of historic [[cultural heritage]] within various areas of Yokohama City—items that have been preserved by local residents and are essential for understanding the region—in order to promote their protection and utilization. Properties registered under this system are also referred to as "Regional Cultural Properties" or "Yokohama City Regional Cultural Properties."
(See also the entry on '''[[Yokohama City Designated Cultural Properties and Regional Cultural Properties List]]'''.)
Following the 1996 (Heisei 8) amendment to the {{ill|Cultural Property Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法}}, a registration system for cultural properties was established in addition to the conventional system for designated tangible cultural properties. This registration system was also extended to tangible [[folk cultural properties]] and [[memorials]] (including [[historical sites]], [[places of scenic beauty]], and [[natural monuments]]). (Under the provisions of the Cultural Property Protection Law, the terms '''"designation"''' and '''"registration"''' are clearly distinguished, so it is incorrect to state on websites or tourist guides that a property is "designated" when it is in fact "registered.")
The Yokohama City Regional Cultural Property system, while similar to the national registration system, was actually established earlier, in 1988 (Shōwa 63). It is designed to complement the designated cultural property system by providing a more "relaxed form of protection"—one that does not impose the same level of legal binding force, relying instead on the understanding and cooperation of local residents.
Properties are registered following reviews held annually by the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kyoiku/shingikai/bunkazai/], which convene each fiscal year based on requests from local history enthusiasts and cultural property owners.

Cultural Property}}[[Category:11]] {{出典の明記|date=2018-02-11}} '''Yokohama City Registered Regional Cultural Property''' (横浜市登録地域文化財) is one of the protection systems for [[registered tangible cultural properties]] uniquely implemented by {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市}} under the provisions of the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001174.html#e000000737] Chapter 6 (Articles 45–49).<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0027_20240105.pdf|accessdate=2024-10-22}}</ref>
[[File:生麦事件碑 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|One example of a City Registered Regional Cultural Property: the Namamugi Incident Monument]]
== Overview == This system allows for the registration of historic [[cultural heritage]] within various areas of Yokohama City—items that have been preserved by local residents and are essential for understanding the region—in order to promote their protection and utilization. Properties registered under this system are also referred to as "Regional Cultural Properties" or "Yokohama City Regional Cultural Properties."
(See also the entry on '''[[Yokohama City Designated Cultural Properties and Regional Cultural Properties List]]'''.)
Following the 1996 (Heisei 8) amendment to the {{ill|Cultural Property Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法}}, a registration system for cultural properties was established in addition to the conventional system for designated tangible cultural properties. This registration system was also extended to tangible [[folk cultural properties]] and [[memorials]] (including [[historical sites]], [[places of scenic beauty]], and [[natural monuments]]). (Under the provisions of the Cultural Property Protection Law, the terms '''"designation"''' and '''"registration"''' are clearly distinguished, so it is incorrect to state on websites or tourist guides that a property is "designated" when it is in fact "registered.")
The Yokohama City Regional Cultural Property system, while similar to the national registration system, was actually established earlier, in 1988 (Shōwa 63). It is designed to complement the designated cultural property system by providing a more "relaxed form of protection"—one that does not impose the same level of legal binding force, relying instead on the understanding and cooperation of local residents.
Properties are registered following reviews held annually by the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kyoiku/shingikai/bunkazai/], which convene each fiscal year based on requests from local history enthusiasts and cultural property owners.

Cultural Property}}[[Category:11]] {{出典の明記|date=2018-02-11}} '''Yokohama City Registered Regional Cultural Property''' (横浜市登録地域文化財) is one of the protection systems for [[registered tangible cultural properties]] uniquely implemented by {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市}} under the provisions of the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001174.html#e000000737] Chapter 6 (Articles 45–49).<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0027_20240105.pdf|accessdate=2024-10-22}}</ref>
[[File:生麦事件碑 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|One example of a City Registered Regional Cultural Property: the Namamugi Incident Monument]]
== Overview == This system allows for the registration of historic [[cultural heritage]] within various areas of Yokohama City—items that have been preserved by local residents and are essential for understanding the region—in order to promote their protection and utilization. Properties registered under this system are also referred to as "Regional Cultural Properties" or "Yokohama City Regional Cultural Properties."
(See also the entry on '''[[Yokohama City Designated Cultural Properties and Regional Cultural Properties List]]'''.)
Following the 1996 (Heisei 8) amendment to the {{ill|Cultural Property Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法}}, a registration system for cultural properties was established in addition to the conventional system for designated tangible cultural properties. This registration system was also extended to tangible [[folk cultural properties]] and [[memorials]] (including [[historical sites]], [[places of scenic beauty]], and [[natural monuments]]). (Under the provisions of the Cultural Property Protection Law, the terms '''"designation"''' and '''"registration"''' are clearly distinguished, so it is incorrect to state on websites or tourist guides that a property is "designated" when it is in fact "registered.")
The Yokohama City Regional Cultural Property system, while similar to the national registration system, was actually established earlier, in 1988 (Shōwa 63). It is designed to complement the designated cultural property system by providing a more "relaxed form of protection"—one that does not impose the same level of legal binding force, relying instead on the understanding and cooperation of local residents.
Properties are registered following reviews held annually by the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kyoiku/shingikai/bunkazai/], which convene each fiscal year based on requests from local history enthusiasts and cultural property owners.

City Registered Cultural Property}}[[Category:11]] {{出典の明記|date=2018-02-11}} '''Yokohama City Registered Regional Cultural Property''' (横浜市登録地域文化財) is one of the protection systems for [[registered tangible cultural properties]] uniquely implemented by {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市}} under the provisions of the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001174.html#e000000737] Chapter 6 (Articles 45–49).<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0027_20240105.pdf|accessdate=2024-10-22}}</ref>
[[File:生麦事件碑 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|One example of a City Registered Regional Cultural Property: the Namamugi Incident Monument]]
== Overview == This system allows for the registration of historic [[cultural heritage]] within various areas of Yokohama City—items that have been preserved by local residents and are essential for understanding the region—in order to promote their protection and utilization. Properties registered under this system are also referred to as "Regional Cultural Properties" or "Yokohama City Regional Cultural Properties."
(See also the entry on '''[[Yokohama City Designated Cultural Properties and Regional Cultural Properties List]]'''.)
Following the 1996 (Heisei 8) amendment to the {{ill|Cultural Property Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法}}, a registration system for cultural properties was established in addition to the conventional system for designated tangible cultural properties. This registration system was also extended to tangible [[folk cultural properties]] and [[memorials]] (including [[historical sites]], [[places of scenic beauty]], and [[natural monuments]]). (Under the provisions of the Cultural Property Protection Law, the terms '''"designation"''' and '''"registration"''' are clearly distinguished, so it is incorrect to state on websites or tourist guides that a property is "designated" when it is in fact "registered.")
The Yokohama City Regional Cultural Property system, while similar to the national registration system, was actually established earlier, in 1988 (Shōwa 63). It is designed to complement the designated cultural property system by providing a more "relaxed form of protection"—one that does not impose the same level of legal binding force, relying instead on the understanding and cooperation of local residents.
Properties are registered following reviews held annually by the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kyoiku/shingikai/bunkazai/], which convene each fiscal year based on requests from local history enthusiasts and cultural property owners.

City Registered Cultural Property}}[[Category:11]] {{出典の明記|date=2018-02-11}} '''Yokohama City Registered Regional Cultural Property''' (横浜市登録地域文化財) is one of the protection systems for [[registered tangible cultural properties]] uniquely implemented by {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市}} under the provisions of the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001174.html#e000000737] Chapter 6 (Articles 45–49).<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0027_20240105.pdf|accessdate=2024-10-22}}</ref>
[[File:生麦事件碑 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|One example of a City Registered Regional Cultural Property: the Namamugi Incident Monument]]
== Overview == This system allows for the registration of historic [[cultural heritage]] within various areas of Yokohama City—items that have been preserved by local residents and are essential for understanding the region—in order to promote their protection and utilization. Properties registered under this system are also referred to as "Regional Cultural Properties" or "Yokohama City Regional Cultural Properties."
(See also the entry on '''[[Yokohama City Designated Cultural Properties and Regional Cultural Properties List]]'''.)
Following the 1996 (Heisei 8) amendment to the {{ill|Cultural Property Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法}}, a registration system for cultural properties was established in addition to the conventional system for designated tangible cultural properties. This registration system was also extended to tangible [[folk cultural properties]] and [[memorials]] (including [[historical sites]], [[places of scenic beauty]], and [[natural monuments]]). (Under the provisions of the Cultural Property Protection Law, the terms '''"designation"''' and '''"registration"''' are clearly distinguished, so it is incorrect to state on websites or tourist guides that a property is "designated" when it is in fact "registered.")
The Yokohama City Regional Cultural Property system, while similar to the national registration system, was actually established earlier, in 1988 (Shōwa 63). It is designed to complement the designated cultural property system by providing a more "relaxed form of protection"—one that does not impose the same level of legal binding force, relying instead on the understanding and cooperation of local residents.
Properties are registered following reviews held annually by the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kyoiku/shingikai/bunkazai/], which convene each fiscal year based on requests from local history enthusiasts and cultural property owners.

[[sn:Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property]]<!--Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property-->[[Category:11]][[sn:Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property}}[[Category:11]] {{出典の明記|date=2018-02-11}} '''Yokohama City Registered Regional Cultural Property''' (横浜市登録地域文化財) is one of the protection systems for [[registered tangible cultural properties]] uniquely implemented by {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市}} under the provisions of the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001174.html#e000000737] Chapter 6 (Articles 45–49).<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0027_20240105.pdf|accessdate=2024-10-22}}</ref>
[[File:生麦事件碑 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|One example of a City Registered Regional Cultural Property: the Namamugi Incident Monument]]
== Overview == This system allows for the registration of historic [[cultural heritage]] within various areas of Yokohama City—items that have been preserved by local residents and are essential for understanding the region—in order to promote their protection and utilization. Properties registered under this system are also referred to as "Regional Cultural Properties" or "Yokohama City Regional Cultural Properties."
(See also the entry on '''[[Yokohama City Designated Cultural Properties and Regional Cultural Properties List]]'''.)
Following the 1996 (Heisei 8) amendment to the {{ill|Cultural Property Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法}}, a registration system for cultural properties was established in addition to the conventional system for designated tangible cultural properties. This registration system was also extended to tangible [[folk cultural properties]] and [[memorials]] (including [[historical sites]], [[places of scenic beauty]], and [[natural monuments]]). (Under the provisions of the Cultural Property Protection Law, the terms '''"designation"''' and '''"registration"''' are clearly distinguished, so it is incorrect to state on websites or tourist guides that a property is "designated" when it is in fact "registered.")
The Yokohama City Regional Cultural Property system, while similar to the national registration system, was actually established earlier, in 1988 (Shōwa 63). It is designed to complement the designated cultural property system by providing a more "relaxed form of protection"—one that does not impose the same level of legal binding force, relying instead on the understanding and cooperation of local residents.
Properties are registered following reviews held annually by the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kyoiku/shingikai/bunkazai/], which convene each fiscal year based on requests from local history enthusiasts and cultural property owners.
== Footnotes ==

== Related Articles == * [[Cultural Property Protection Law]] * [[Registered Tangible Cultural Property]] * [[Cultural Property]] * [[Yokohama City Designated Cultural Properties and Regional Cultural Properties List]] * [[Buried Cultural Properties]] * [[Kawasaki City Regional Cultural Properties]] – A similar system in [[Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Kawasaki City]]. * [[Chiba City Regional Cultural Properties]] – A similar system in [[Chiba Prefecture]] [[Chiba City]].

City Registered Cultural Property}}[[Category:11]] {{出典の明記|date=2018-02-11}} '''Yokohama City Registered Regional Cultural Property''' (横浜市登録地域文化財) is one of the protection systems for [[registered tangible cultural properties]] uniquely implemented by {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市}} under the provisions of the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001174.html#e000000737] Chapter 6 (Articles 45–49).<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0027_20240105.pdf|accessdate=2024-10-22}}</ref>
[[File:生麦事件碑 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|One example of a City Registered Regional Cultural Property: the Namamugi Incident Monument]]
== Overview == This system allows for the registration of historic [[cultural heritage]] within various areas of Yokohama City—items that have been preserved by local residents and are essential for understanding the region—in order to promote their protection and utilization. Properties registered under this system are also referred to as "Regional Cultural Properties" or "Yokohama City Regional Cultural Properties."
(See also the entry on '''[[Yokohama City Designated Cultural Properties and Regional Cultural Properties List]]'''.)
Following the 1996 (Heisei 8) amendment to the {{ill|Cultural Property Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法}}, a registration system for cultural properties was established in addition to the conventional system for designated tangible cultural properties. This registration system was also extended to tangible [[folk cultural properties]] and [[memorials]] (including [[historical sites]], [[places of scenic beauty]], and [[natural monuments]]). (Under the provisions of the Cultural Property Protection Law, the terms '''"designation"''' and '''"registration"''' are clearly distinguished, so it is incorrect to state on websites or tourist guides that a property is "designated" when it is in fact "registered.")
The Yokohama City Regional Cultural Property system, while similar to the national registration system, was actually established earlier, in 1988 (Shōwa 63). It is designed to complement the designated cultural property system by providing a more "relaxed form of protection"—one that does not impose the same level of legal binding force, relying instead on the understanding and cooperation of local residents.
Properties are registered following reviews held annually by the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kyoiku/shingikai/bunkazai/], which convene each fiscal year based on requests from local history enthusiasts and cultural property owners.

Cultural Property}}[[Category:11]] {{出典の明記|date=2018-02-11}} '''Yokohama City Registered Regional Cultural Property''' (横浜市登録地域文化財) is one of the protection systems for [[registered tangible cultural properties]] uniquely implemented by {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市}} under the provisions of the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001174.html#e000000737] Chapter 6 (Articles 45–49).<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0027_20240105.pdf|accessdate=2024-10-22}}</ref>
[[File:生麦事件碑 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|One example of a City Registered Regional Cultural Property: the Namamugi Incident Monument]]
== Overview == This system allows for the registration of historic [[cultural heritage]] within various areas of Yokohama City—items that have been preserved by local residents and are essential for understanding the region—in order to promote their protection and utilization. Properties registered under this system are also referred to as "Regional Cultural Properties" or "Yokohama City Regional Cultural Properties."
(See also the entry on '''[[Yokohama City Designated Cultural Properties and Regional Cultural Properties List]]'''.)
Following the 1996 (Heisei 8) amendment to the {{ill|Cultural Property Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法}}, a registration system for cultural properties was established in addition to the conventional system for designated tangible cultural properties. This registration system was also extended to tangible [[folk cultural properties]] and [[memorials]] (including [[historical sites]], [[places of scenic beauty]], and [[natural monuments]]). (Under the provisions of the Cultural Property Protection Law, the terms '''"designation"''' and '''"registration"''' are clearly distinguished, so it is incorrect to state on websites or tourist guides that a property is "designated" when it is in fact "registered.")
The Yokohama City Regional Cultural Property system, while similar to the national registration system, was actually established earlier, in 1988 (Shōwa 63). It is designed to complement the designated cultural property system by providing a more "relaxed form of protection"—one that does not impose the same level of legal binding force, relying instead on the understanding and cooperation of local residents.
Properties are registered following reviews held annually by the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kyoiku/shingikai/bunkazai/], which convene each fiscal year based on requests from local history enthusiasts and cultural property owners.

<!--Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property--> {{出典の明記|date=2018-02-11}} '''Yokohama City Registered Regional Cultural Property''' (横浜市登録地域文化財) is one of the protection systems for [[registered tangible cultural properties]] uniquely implemented by {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市}} under the provisions of the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001174.html#e000000737] Chapter 6 (Articles 45–49).<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0027_20240105.pdf|accessdate=2024-10-22}}</ref>
[[File:生麦事件碑 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|One example of a City Registered Regional Cultural Property: the Namamugi Incident Monument]]
== Overview == This system allows for the registration of historic [[cultural heritage]] within various areas of Yokohama City—items that have been preserved by local residents and are essential for understanding the region—in order to promote their protection and utilization. Properties registered under this system are also referred to as "Regional Cultural Properties" or "Yokohama City Regional Cultural Properties."
(See also the entry on '''[[Yokohama City Designated Cultural Properties and Regional Cultural Properties List]]'''.)
Following the 1996 (Heisei 8) amendment to the {{ill|Cultural Property Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法}}, a registration system for cultural properties was established in addition to the conventional system for designated tangible cultural properties. This registration system was also extended to tangible [[folk cultural properties]] and [[memorials]] (including [[historical sites]], [[places of scenic beauty]], and [[natural monuments]]). (Under the provisions of the Cultural Property Protection Law, the terms '''"designation"''' and '''"registration"''' are clearly distinguished, so it is incorrect to state on websites or tourist guides that a property is "designated" when it is in fact "registered.")
The Yokohama City Regional Cultural Property system, while similar to the national registration system, was actually established earlier, in 1988 (Shōwa 63). It is designed to complement the designated cultural property system by providing a more "relaxed form of protection"—one that does not impose the same level of legal binding force, relying instead on the understanding and cooperation of local residents.
Properties are registered following reviews held annually by the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kyoiku/shingikai/bunkazai/], which convene each fiscal year based on requests from local history enthusiasts and cultural property owners.
== Footnotes ==
{{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * [[Cultural Property Protection Law]] * [[Registered Tangible Cultural Property]] * [[Cultural Property]] * [[Yokohama City Designated Cultural Properties and Regional Cultural Properties List]] * [[Buried Cultural Properties]] * [[Kawasaki City Regional Cultural Properties]] – A similar system in [[Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Kawasaki City]]. * [[Chiba City Regional Cultural Properties]] – A similar system in [[Chiba Prefecture]] [[Chiba City]].
== External Links == * [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001174.html Yokohama City Cultural Property Protection Ordinance] * [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001175.html Yokohama City Cultural Property Protection Ordinance Enforcement Regulations] * [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kyoiku/shingikai/bunkazai/ Yokohama City Cultural Property Protection Council]
{{citation needed|よこはましとうろくちいきぶんかざい}}

revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2018-02-11}} '''Yokohama City Registered Regional Cultural Property''' (横浜市登録地域文化財) is one of the protection systems for [[registered tangible cultural properties]] uniquely implemented by {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市|12=draft}} under the provisions of the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001174.html#e000000737] Chapter 6 (Articles 45–49).<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会|12=draft}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0027_20240105.pdf|accessdate=2024-10-22}}</ref>
[[File:生麦事件碑 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|One example of a City Registered Regional Cultural Property: the Namamugi Incident Monument]]
== Overview == This system allows for the registration of historic [[cultural heritage]] within various areas of Yokohama City—items that have been preserved by local residents and are essential for understanding the region—in order to promote their protection and utilization. Properties registered under this system are also referred to as "Regional Cultural Properties" or "Yokohama City Regional Cultural Properties."
(See also the entry on '''[[Yokohama City Designated Cultural Properties and Regional Cultural Properties List]]'''.)
Following the 1996 (Heisei 8) amendment to the {{ill|Cultural Property Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法|12=draft}}, a registration system for cultural properties was established in addition to the conventional system for designated tangible cultural properties. This registration system was also extended to tangible [[folk cultural properties]] and [[memorials]] (including [[historical sites]], [[places of scenic beauty]], and [[natural monuments]]). (Under the provisions of the Cultural Property Protection Law, the terms '''"designation"''' and '''"registration"''' are clearly distinguished, so it is incorrect to state on websites or tourist guides that a property is "designated" when it is in fact "registered.")
The Yokohama City Regional Cultural Property system, while similar to the national registration system, was actually established earlier, in 1988 (Shōwa 63). It is designed to complement the designated cultural property system by providing a more "relaxed form of protection"—one that does not impose the same level of legal binding force, relying instead on the understanding and cooperation of local residents.
Properties are registered following reviews held annually by the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kyoiku/shingikai/bunkazai/], which convene each fiscal year based on requests from local history enthusiasts and cultural property owners.
== Footnotes ==
{{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * [[Cultural Property Protection Law]] * [[Registered Tangible Cultural Property]] * [[Cultural Property]] * [[Yokohama City Designated Cultural Properties and Regional Cultural Properties List]] * [[Buried Cultural Properties]] * [[Kawasaki City Regional Cultural Properties]] – A similar system in [[Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Kawasaki City]]. * [[Chiba City Regional Cultural Properties]] – A similar system in [[Chiba Prefecture]] [[Chiba City]].
== External Links == * [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001174.html Yokohama City Cultural Property Protection Ordinance] * [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001175.html Yokohama City Cultural Property Protection Ordinance Enforcement Regulations] * [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kyoiku/shingikai/bunkazai/ Yokohama City Cultural Property Protection Council]
{{citation needed|よこはましとうろくちいきぶんかざい}}
