{{出典の明記|date=2018-02-11}} '''Yokohama City Registered Regional Cultural Property''' (横浜市登録地域文化財) is one of the protection systems for [[registered tangible cultural properties]] uniquely implemented by {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市|12=draft|12=draft}} under the provisions of the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001174.html#e000000737] Chapter 6 (Articles 45–49).<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会|12=draft|12=draft}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0027_20240105.pdf|accessdate=2024-10-22}}</ref>
[[File:生麦事件碑 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|One example of a City Registered Regional Cultural Property: the Namamugi Incident Monument]]
== Overview == This system allows for the registration of historic [[cultural heritage]] within various areas of Yokohama City—items that have been preserved by local residents and are essential for understanding the region—in order to promote their protection and utilization. Properties registered under this system are also referred to as "Regional Cultural Properties" or "Yokohama City Regional Cultural Properties."
(See also the entry on '''[[Yokohama City Designated Cultural Properties and Regional Cultural Properties List]]'''.)
Following the 1996 (Heisei 8) amendment to the {{ill|Cultural Property Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法|12=draft|12=draft}}, a registration system for cultural properties was established in addition to the conventional system for designated tangible cultural properties. This registration system was also extended to tangible [[folk cultural properties]] and [[memorials]] (including [[historical sites]], [[places of scenic beauty]], and [[natural monuments]]). (Under the provisions of the Cultural Property Protection Law, the terms '''"designation"''' and '''"registration"''' are clearly distinguished, so it is incorrect to state on websites or tourist guides that a property is "designated" when it is in fact "registered.")
The Yokohama City Regional Cultural Property system, while similar to the national registration system, was actually established earlier, in 1988 (Shōwa 63). It is designed to complement the designated cultural property system by providing a more "relaxed form of protection"—one that does not impose the same level of legal binding force, relying instead on the understanding and cooperation of local residents.
Properties are registered following reviews held annually by the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kyoiku/shingikai/bunkazai/], which convene each fiscal year based on requests from local history enthusiasts and cultural property owners.

revision.comment: 17 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:横浜市登録地域文化財 up to rev 102300550
revision.text:
'''横浜市登録地域文化財'''（よこはましとうろくちいきぶんかざい）は、[http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001174.html#e000000737 横浜市文化財保護条例第6章（第45条～第49条）]の規定によって[[横浜市]]が独自に施行する[[登録有形文化財|登録文化財]]保護制度の一つである<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher=[[横浜市教育委員会]]|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0027_20240105.pdf|accessdate=2024-10-22}}</ref>。
[[File:生麦事件碑 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|市登録地域文化財の一つ、生麦事件碑]]
== 概要 == 横浜市内の各地域における歴史的[[文化遺産]]について、地域住民が守ってきたもの、地域を知る上で必要なものを選定し、その保護・活用を図るため、横浜市登録地域文化財として登録することができる。「地域文化財」・「横浜市地域文化財」と呼ばれることもある。
（「'''[[横浜市指定文化財および地域文化財一覧]]'''」の項も参照）
1996年（平成8年）の[[文化財保護法]]改正により、従来の[[指定文化財|指定有形文化財]]制度に加えて、文化財'''「[[登録有形文化財|登録]]」'''制度が創設された。有形[[民俗文化財]]、[[記念物]]（[[史跡]]・[[名勝]]・[[天然記念物]]）についても、登録制度が導入された。（[[文化財保護法]]の規定上、'''「指定」'''と'''「登録」'''とは明確に区別されているため、ウェブサイトや観光案内書等で「'''登録'''有形文化財として'''指定'''されている」などと表記するのは誤りである。）
本市地域文化財も、この登録制度に近いものであるが、創設時期は国の登録文化財制度よりも古い1988年（昭和63年）である。指定文化財制度を補完し、指定文化財制度ほどの[[法的拘束力]]を与えず、地域住民の理解と協力により文化財を保護していくと言う'''「緩やかな保護」'''を目的とする制度である。
市内各区や地域の歴史愛好家や文化財の所有者からの要望を受け、各年度ごとに開かれる[http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kyoiku/shingikai/bunkazai/ 横浜市文化財保護審議会]の審議を経て登録される。
==脚注== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[文化財保護法]] * [[登録有形文化財]] * [[文化財]] * [[横浜市指定文化財および地域文化財一覧]] * [[埋蔵文化財]] * [[川崎市地域文化財]] - [[神奈川県]][[川崎市]]にあるよく似た制度。 * [[千葉市地域文化財]] - [[千葉県]][[千葉市]]にあるよく似た制度。
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001174.html 横浜市文化財保護条例] * [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001175.html 横浜市文化財保護条例施行規則] * [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kyoiku/shingikai/bunkazai/ 横浜市文化財保護審議会]
{{デフォルトソート:よこはましとうろくちいきふんかさい}} [[Category:横浜市の歴史]] [[Category:日本の文化遺産保護制度]]

1996年（平成8年）の[[文化財保護法]]改正により、従来の[[指定文化財|指定有形文化財]]制度に加えて、文化財'''「[[登録有形文化財|登録]]」'''制度が創設された。有形[[民俗文化財]]、[[記念物]]（[[史跡]]・[[名勝]]・[[天然記念物]]）についても、登録制度が導入された。（[[文化財保護法]]の規定上、'''「指定」'''と'''「登録」'''とは明確に区別されているため、ウェブサイトや観光案内書等で「'''登録'''有形文化財として'''指定'''されている」などと表記するのは誤りである。）
本市地域文化財も、この登録制度に近いものであるが、創設時期は国の登録文化財制度よりも古い1988年（昭和63年）である。指定文化財制度を補完し、指定文化財制度ほどの[[法的拘束力]]を与えず、地域住民の理解と協力により文化財を保護していくと言う'''「緩やかな保護」'''を目的とする制度である。
市内各区や地域の歴史愛好家や文化財の所有者からの要望を受け、各年度ごとに開かれる[http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kyoiku/shingikai/bunkazai/ 横浜市文化財保護審議会]の審議を経て登録される。

{{出典の明記|date=2018-02-11}} '''Yokohama City Registered Regional Cultural Property''' (横浜市登録地域文化財) is one of the protection systems for [[registered tangible cultural properties]] uniquely implemented by {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under the provisions of the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001174.html#e000000737] Chapter 6 (Articles 45–49).<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0027_20240105.pdf|accessdate=2024-10-22}}</ref>
[[File:生麦事件碑 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|One example of a City Registered Regional Cultural Property: the Namamugi Incident Monument]]
== Overview == This system allows for the registration of historic [[cultural heritage]] within various areas of Yokohama City—items that have been preserved by local residents and are essential for understanding the region—in order to promote their protection and utilization. Properties registered under this system are also referred to as "Regional Cultural Properties" or "Yokohama City Regional Cultural Properties."
(See also the entry on '''[[Yokohama City Designated Cultural Properties and Regional Cultural Properties List]]'''.)
Following the 1996 (Heisei 8) amendment to the {{ill|Cultural Property Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a registration system for cultural properties was established in addition to the conventional system for designated tangible cultural properties. This registration system was also extended to tangible [[folk cultural properties]] and [[memorials]] (including [[historical sites]], [[places of scenic beauty]], and [[natural monuments]]). (Under the provisions of the Cultural Property Protection Law, the terms '''"designation"''' and '''"registration"''' are clearly distinguished, so it is incorrect to state on websites or tourist guides that a property is "designated" when it is in fact "registered.")
The Yokohama City Regional Cultural Property system, while similar to the national registration system, was actually established earlier, in 1988 (Shōwa 63). It is designed to complement the designated cultural property system by providing a more "relaxed form of protection"—one that does not impose the same level of legal binding force, relying instead on the understanding and cooperation of local residents.
Properties are registered following reviews held annually by the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kyoiku/shingikai/bunkazai/], which convene each fiscal year based on requests from local history enthusiasts and cultural property owners.

revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:日本の文化遺産保護制度]] from page
revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2018-02-11}} '''Yokohama City Registered Regional Cultural Property''' (横浜市登録地域文化財) is one of the protection systems for [[registered tangible cultural properties]] uniquely implemented by {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under the provisions of the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001174.html#e000000737] Chapter 6 (Articles 45–49).<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0027_20240105.pdf|accessdate=2024-10-22}}</ref>
[[File:生麦事件碑 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|One example of a City Registered Regional Cultural Property: the Namamugi Incident Monument]]
== Overview == This system allows for the registration of historic [[cultural heritage]] within various areas of Yokohama City—items that have been preserved by local residents and are essential for understanding the region—in order to promote their protection and utilization. Properties registered under this system are also referred to as "Regional Cultural Properties" or "Yokohama City Regional Cultural Properties."
(See also the entry on '''[[Yokohama City Designated Cultural Properties and Regional Cultural Properties List]]'''.)
Following the 1996 (Heisei 8) amendment to the {{ill|Cultural Property Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a registration system for cultural properties was established in addition to the conventional system for designated tangible cultural properties. This registration system was also extended to tangible [[folk cultural properties]] and [[memorials]] (including [[historical sites]], [[places of scenic beauty]], and [[natural monuments]]). (Under the provisions of the Cultural Property Protection Law, the terms '''"designation"''' and '''"registration"''' are clearly distinguished, so it is incorrect to state on websites or tourist guides that a property is "designated" when it is in fact "registered.")
The Yokohama City Regional Cultural Property system, while similar to the national registration system, was actually established earlier, in 1988 (Shōwa 63). It is designed to complement the designated cultural property system by providing a more "relaxed form of protection"—one that does not impose the same level of legal binding force, relying instead on the understanding and cooperation of local residents.
Properties are registered following reviews held annually by the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kyoiku/shingikai/bunkazai/], which convene each fiscal year based on requests from local history enthusiasts and cultural property owners.
== Footnotes ==
{{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * [[Cultural Property Protection Law]] * [[Registered Tangible Cultural Property]] * [[Cultural Property]] * [[Yokohama City Designated Cultural Properties and Regional Cultural Properties List]] * [[Buried Cultural Properties]] * [[Kawasaki City Regional Cultural Properties]] – A similar system in [[Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Kawasaki City]]. * [[Chiba City Regional Cultural Properties]] – A similar system in [[Chiba Prefecture]] [[Chiba City]].
== External Links == * [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001174.html Yokohama City Cultural Property Protection Ordinance] * [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001175.html Yokohama City Cultural Property Protection Ordinance Enforcement Regulations] * [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kyoiku/shingikai/bunkazai/ Yokohama City Cultural Property Protection Council]
{{translated page|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|version=102300550|comment=Imported full ja history}}

}}

revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:Japanese cultural heritage protection system
revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2018-02-11}} '''Yokohama City Registered Regional Cultural Property''' (横浜市登録地域文化財) is one of the protection systems for [[registered tangible cultural properties]] uniquely implemented by [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]] under the provisions of the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001174.html#e000000737] Chapter 6 (Articles 45–49).<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Board of Education|qq=}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0027_20240105.pdf|accessdate=2024-10-22}}</ref>
[[File:生麦事件碑 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|One example of a City Registered Regional Cultural Property: the Namamugi Incident Monument]]
== Overview == This system allows for the registration of historic [[cultural heritage]] within various areas of Yokohama City—items that have been preserved by local residents and are essential for understanding the region—in order to promote their protection and utilization. Properties registered under this system are also referred to as "Regional Cultural Properties" or "Yokohama City Regional Cultural Properties."
(See also the entry on '''[[Yokohama City Designated Cultural Properties and Regional Cultural Properties List]]'''.)
Following the 1996 (Heisei 8) amendment to the {{ill|Cultural Property Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural Property Protection Law|qq=}}, a registration system for cultural properties was established in addition to the conventional system for designated tangible cultural properties. This registration system was also extended to tangible [[folk cultural properties]] and [[memorials]] (including [[historical sites]], [[places of scenic beauty]], and [[natural monuments]]). (Under the provisions of the Cultural Property Protection Law, the terms '''"designation"''' and '''"registration"''' are clearly distinguished, so it is incorrect to state on websites or tourist guides that a property is "designated" when it is in fact "registered.")
The Yokohama City Regional Cultural Property system, while similar to the national registration system, was actually established earlier, in 1988 (Shōwa 63). It is designed to complement the designated cultural property system by providing a more "relaxed form of protection"—one that does not impose the same level of legal binding force, relying instead on the understanding and cooperation of local residents.
Properties are registered following reviews held annually by the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kyoiku/shingikai/bunkazai/], which convene each fiscal year based on requests from local history enthusiasts and cultural property owners.
== Footnotes ==
{{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * [[Cultural Property Protection Law]] * [[Registered Tangible Cultural Property]] * [[Cultural Property]] * [[Yokohama City Designated Cultural Properties and Regional Cultural Properties List]] * [[Buried Cultural Properties]] * [[Kawasaki City Regional Cultural Properties]] – A similar system in [[Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Kawasaki City]]. * [[Chiba City Regional Cultural Properties]] – A similar system in [[Chiba Prefecture]] [[Chiba City]].
== External Links == * [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001174.html Yokohama City Cultural Property Protection Ordinance] * [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001175.html Yokohama City Cultural Property Protection Ordinance Enforcement Regulations] * [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kyoiku/shingikai/bunkazai/ Yokohama City Cultural Property Protection Council]

Properties are registered following reviews held annually by the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kyoiku/shingikai/bunkazai/], which convene each fiscal year based on requests from local history enthusiasts and cultural property owners.

{{出典の明記|date=2018-02-11}} '''Yokohama City Registered Regional Cultural Property''' (横浜市登録地域文化財) is one of the protection systems for [[registered tangible cultural properties]] uniquely implemented by [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]] under the provisions of the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001174.html#e000000737] Chapter 6 (Articles 45–49).<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Board of Education|qq=}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0027_20240105.pdf|accessdate=2024-10-22}}</ref>
[[File:生麦事件碑 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|One example of a City Registered Regional Cultural Property: the Namamugi Incident Monument]]
== Overview == This system allows for the registration of historic [[cultural heritage]] within various areas of Yokohama City—items that have been preserved by local residents and are essential for understanding the region—in order to promote their protection and utilization. Properties registered under this system are also referred to as "Regional Cultural Properties" or "Yokohama City Regional Cultural Properties."
(See also the entry on '''[[Yokohama City Designated Cultural Properties and Regional Cultural Properties List]]'''.)
Following the 1996 (Heisei 8) amendment to the {{ill|Cultural Property Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural Property Protection Law|qq=}}, a registration system for cultural properties was established in addition to the conventional system for designated tangible cultural properties. This registration system was also extended to tangible [[folk cultural properties]] and [[memorials]] (including [[historical sites]], [[places of scenic beauty]], and [[natural monuments]]). (Under the provisions of the Cultural Property Protection Law, the terms '''"designation"''' and '''"registration"''' are clearly distinguished, so it is incorrect to state on websites or tourist guides that a property is "designated" when it is in fact "registered.")
The Yokohama City Regional Cultural Property system, while similar to the national registration system, was actually established earlier, in 1988 (Shōwa 63). It is designed to complement the designated cultural property system by providing a more "relaxed form of protection"—one that does not impose the same level of legal binding force, relying instead on the understanding and cooperation of local residents.
Properties are registered following reviews held annually by the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kyoiku/shingikai/bunkazai/], which convene each fiscal year based on requests from local history enthusiasts and cultural property owners.

Following the 1996 (Heisei 8) amendment to the {{ill|Cultural Property Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural Property Protection Law|qq=}}, a registration system for cultural properties was established in addition to the conventional system for designated tangible cultural properties. This registration system was also extended to tangible [[folk cultural properties]] and [[memorials]] (including [[historical sites]], [[places of scenic beauty]], and [[natural monuments]]). (Under the provisions of the Cultural Property Protection Law, the terms '''"designation"''' and '''"registration"''' are clearly distinguished, so it is incorrect to state on websites or tourist guides that a property is "designated" when it is in fact "registered.")
The Yokohama City Regional Cultural Property system, while similar to the national registration system, was actually established earlier, in 1988 (Shōwa 63). It is designed to complement the designated cultural property system by providing a more "relaxed form of protection"—one that does not impose the same level of legal binding force, relying instead on the understanding and cooperation of local residents.
Properties are registered following reviews held annually by the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kyoiku/shingikai/bunkazai/], which convene each fiscal year based on requests from local history enthusiasts and cultural property owners.

{{出典の明記|date=2018-02-11}} '''Yokohama City Registered Regional Cultural Property''' (横浜市登録地域文化財) is one of the protection systems for [[registered tangible cultural properties]] uniquely implemented by [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]] under the provisions of the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001174.html#e000000737] Chapter 6 (Articles 45–49).<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Board of Education|qq=}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0027_20240105.pdf|accessdate=2024-10-22}}</ref>
[[File:生麦事件碑 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|One example of a City Registered Regional Cultural Property: the Namamugi Incident Monument]]
== Overview == This system allows for the registration of historic [[:en:Cultural heritage|Cultural heritage]] within various areas of Yokohama City—items that have been preserved by local residents and are essential for understanding the region—in order to promote their protection and utilization. Properties registered under this system are also referred to as "Regional Cultural Properties" or "Yokohama City Regional Cultural Properties."
(See also the entry on '''[[Yokohama City Designated Cultural Properties and Regional Cultural Properties List]]'''.)
Following the 1996 (Heisei 8) amendment to the {{ill|Cultural Property Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural Property Protection Law|qq=}}, a registration system for cultural properties was established in addition to the conventional system for designated tangible cultural properties. This registration system was also extended to tangible [[folk cultural properties]] and [[:en:Memorials|Memorials]] (including [[historical sites]], [[places of scenic beauty]], and [[natural monuments]]). (Under the provisions of the Cultural Property Protection Law, the terms '''"designation"''' and '''"registration"''' are clearly distinguished, so it is incorrect to state on websites or tourist guides that a property is "designated" when it is in fact "registered.")
The Yokohama City Regional Cultural Property system, while similar to the national registration system, was actually established earlier, in 1988 (Shōwa 63). It is designed to complement the designated cultural property system by providing a more "relaxed form of protection"—one that does not impose the same level of legal binding force, relying instead on the understanding and cooperation of local residents.
Properties are registered following reviews held annually by the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kyoiku/shingikai/bunkazai/], which convene each fiscal year based on requests from local history enthusiasts and cultural property owners.
== Footnotes ==
{{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * [[Cultural Property Protection Law]] * [[Registered Tangible Cultural Property]] * [[:en:Cultural Property|Cultural Property]] * [[Yokohama City Designated Cultural Properties and Regional Cultural Properties List]] * [[:en:Buried Cultural Properties|Buried Cultural Properties]] * [[Kawasaki City Regional Cultural Properties]] – A similar system in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[:en:Kawasaki City|Kawasaki City]]. * [[Chiba City Regional Cultural Properties]] – A similar system in [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]] [[:en:Chiba City|Chiba City]].
== External Links == * [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001174.html Yokohama City Cultural Property Protection Ordinance] * [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001175.html Yokohama City Cultural Property Protection Ordinance Enforcement Regulations] * [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kyoiku/shingikai/bunkazai/ Yokohama City Cultural Property Protection Council]
{{translated page|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|version=102300550|comment=Imported full ja history}}
{{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]]
}}

[[Category:Japanese cultural heritage protection system]] [[Category:History of Yokohama]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property]][[Category:qq]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q30928910]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{出典の明記|date=2018-02-11}} '''Yokohama City Registered Regional Cultural Property''' (横浜市登録地域文化財) is one of the protection systems for [[registered tangible cultural properties]] uniquely implemented by [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]] under the provisions of the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001174.html#e000000737] Chapter 6 (Articles 45–49).<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Board of Education|qq=|1=Yokohama City Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543020}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0027_20240105.pdf|accessdate=2024-10-22}}</ref>
[[File:生麦事件碑 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|One example of a City Registered Regional Cultural Property: the Namamugi Incident Monument]]
== Overview == This system allows for the registration of historic [[:en:Cultural heritage|Cultural heritage]] within various areas of Yokohama City—items that have been preserved by local residents and are essential for understanding the region—in order to promote their protection and utilization. Properties registered under this system are also referred to as "Regional Cultural Properties" or "Yokohama City Regional Cultural Properties."
(See also the entry on '''[[Yokohama City Designated Cultural Properties and Regional Cultural Properties List]]'''.)
Following the 1996 (Heisei 8) amendment to the {{ill|Cultural Property Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural Property Protection Law|qq=|1=Act on Protection of Cultural Properties|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1188502}}, a registration system for cultural properties was established in addition to the conventional system for designated tangible cultural properties. This registration system was also extended to tangible [[folk cultural properties]] and [[:en:Memorials|Memorials]] (including [[historical sites]], [[places of scenic beauty]], and [[natural monuments]]). (Under the provisions of the Cultural Property Protection Law, the terms '''"designation"''' and '''"registration"''' are clearly distinguished, so it is incorrect to state on websites or tourist guides that a property is "designated" when it is in fact "registered.")
The Yokohama City Regional Cultural Property system, while similar to the national registration system, was actually established earlier, in 1988 (Shōwa 63). It is designed to complement the designated cultural property system by providing a more "relaxed form of protection"—one that does not impose the same level of legal binding force, relying instead on the understanding and cooperation of local residents.
Properties are registered following reviews held annually by the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kyoiku/shingikai/bunkazai/], which convene each fiscal year based on requests from local history enthusiasts and cultural property owners.

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q30928910]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{出典の明記|date=2018-02-11}} '''Yokohama City Registered Regional Cultural Property''' (横浜市登録地域文化財) is one of the protection systems for [[registered tangible cultural properties]] uniquely implemented by [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]] under the provisions of the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001174.html#e000000737] Chapter 6 (Articles 45–49).<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Board of Education|qq=|1=Yokohama City Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543020}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0027_20240105.pdf|accessdate=2024-10-22}}</ref>
[[File:生麦事件碑 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|One example of a City Registered Regional Cultural Property: the Namamugi Incident Monument]]
== Overview == This system allows for the registration of historic [[:en:Cultural heritage|Cultural heritage]] within various areas of Yokohama City—items that have been preserved by local residents and are essential for understanding the region—in order to promote their protection and utilization. Properties registered under this system are also referred to as "Regional Cultural Properties" or "Yokohama City Regional Cultural Properties."
(See also the entry on '''[[Yokohama City Designated Cultural Properties and Regional Cultural Properties List]]'''.)
Following the 1996 (Heisei 8) amendment to the {{ill|Cultural Property Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural Property Protection Law|qq=|1=Act on Protection of Cultural Properties|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1188502}}, a registration system for cultural properties was established in addition to the conventional system for designated tangible cultural properties. This registration system was also extended to tangible [[folk cultural properties]] and [[:en:Memorials|Memorials]] (including [[historical sites]], [[places of scenic beauty]], and [[natural monuments]]). (Under the provisions of the Cultural Property Protection Law, the terms '''"designation"''' and '''"registration"''' are clearly distinguished, so it is incorrect to state on websites or tourist guides that a property is "designated" when it is in fact "registered.")
The Yokohama City Regional Cultural Property system, while similar to the national registration system, was actually established earlier, in 1988 (Shōwa 63). It is designed to complement the designated cultural property system by providing a more "relaxed form of protection"—one that does not impose the same level of legal binding force, relying instead on the understanding and cooperation of local residents.
Properties are registered following reviews held annually by the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kyoiku/shingikai/bunkazai/], which convene each fiscal year based on requests from local history enthusiasts and cultural property owners.
== Footnotes ==
{{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * [[Cultural Property Protection Law]] * [[Registered Tangible Cultural Property]] * [[:en:Cultural Property|Cultural Property]] * [[Yokohama City Designated Cultural Properties and Regional Cultural Properties List]] * [[:en:Buried Cultural Properties|Buried Cultural Properties]] * [[Kawasaki City Regional Cultural Properties]] – A similar system in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[:en:Kawasaki City|Kawasaki City]]. * [[Chiba City Regional Cultural Properties]] – A similar system in [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]] [[:en:Chiba City|Chiba City]].
== External Links == * [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001174.html Yokohama City Cultural Property Protection Ordinance] * [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001175.html Yokohama City Cultural Property Protection Ordinance Enforcement Regulations] * [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kyoiku/shingikai/bunkazai/ Yokohama City Cultural Property Protection Council]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q30928910]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{出典の明記|date=2018-02-11}} '''Yokohama City Registered Regional Cultural Property''' (横浜市登録地域文化財) is one of the protection systems for [[registered tangible cultural properties]] uniquely implemented by [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]] under the provisions of the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001174.html#e000000737] Chapter 6 (Articles 45–49).<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Board of Education|qq=|1=Yokohama City Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543020}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0027_20240105.pdf|accessdate=2024-10-22}}</ref>
[[File:生麦事件碑 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|One example of a City Registered Regional Cultural Property: the Namamugi Incident Monument]]
== Overview == This system allows for the registration of historic [[:en:Cultural heritage|Cultural heritage]] within various areas of Yokohama City—items that have been preserved by local residents and are essential for understanding the region—in order to promote their protection and utilization. Properties registered under this system are also referred to as "Regional Cultural Properties" or "Yokohama City Regional Cultural Properties."
(See also the entry on '''[[Yokohama City Designated Cultural Properties and Regional Cultural Properties List]]'''.)
Following the 1996 (Heisei 8) amendment to the {{ill|Cultural Property Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural Property Protection Law|qq=|1=Act on Protection of Cultural Properties|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1188502}}, a registration system for cultural properties was established in addition to the conventional system for designated tangible cultural properties. This registration system was also extended to tangible [[folk cultural properties]] and [[:en:Memorials|Memorials]] (including [[historical sites]], [[places of scenic beauty]], and [[natural monuments]]). (Under the provisions of the Cultural Property Protection Law, the terms '''"designation"''' and '''"registration"''' are clearly distinguished, so it is incorrect to state on websites or tourist guides that a property is "designated" when it is in fact "registered.")
The Yokohama City Regional Cultural Property system, while similar to the national registration system, was actually established earlier, in 1988 (Shōwa 63). It is designed to complement the designated cultural property system by providing a more "relaxed form of protection"—one that does not impose the same level of legal binding force, relying instead on the understanding and cooperation of local residents.
Properties are registered following reviews held annually by the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kyoiku/shingikai/bunkazai/], which convene each fiscal year based on requests from local history enthusiasts and cultural property owners.
== Footnotes ==
{{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * [[Cultural Property Protection Law]] * [[Registered Tangible Cultural Property]] * [[:en:Cultural Property|Cultural Property]] * [[Yokohama City Designated Cultural Properties and Regional Cultural Properties List]] * [[:en:Buried Cultural Properties|Buried Cultural Properties]] * [[Kawasaki City Regional Cultural Properties]] – A similar system in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[:en:Kawasaki City|Kawasaki City]]. * [[Chiba City Regional Cultural Properties]] – A similar system in [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]] [[:en:Chiba City|Chiba City]].
== External Links == * [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001174.html Yokohama City Cultural Property Protection Ordinance] * [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001175.html Yokohama City Cultural Property Protection Ordinance Enforcement Regulations] * [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kyoiku/shingikai/bunkazai/ Yokohama City Cultural Property Protection Council]
{{translated page|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|version=102300550|comment=Imported full ja history}}
{{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]]
}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q30928910]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Referencesの明記|date=2018-02-11}} '''Yokohama City Registered Regional Cultural Property''' (横浜市登録地域文化財) is one of the protection systems for [[registered tangible cultural properties]] uniquely implemented by [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]] under the provisions of the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001174.html#e000000737] Chapter 6 (Articles 45–49).<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Board of Education|1=Yokohama City Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543020}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0027_20240105.pdf|accessdate=2024-10-22}}</ref>
[[File:生麦事件碑 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|One example of a City Registered Regional Cultural Property: the Namamugi Incident Monument]]
== Overview == This system allows for the registration of historic [[:en:Cultural heritage|Cultural heritage]] within various areas of Yokohama City—items that have been preserved by local residents and are essential for understanding the region—in order to promote their protection and utilization. Properties registered under this system are also referred to as "Regional Cultural Properties" or "Yokohama City Regional Cultural Properties."
(See also the entry on '''[[Yokohama City Designated Cultural Properties and Regional Cultural Properties List]]'''.)
Following the 1996 (Heisei 8) amendment to the {{ill|Cultural Property Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural Property Protection Law|1=Act on Protection of Cultural Properties|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1188502}}, a registration system for cultural properties was established in addition to the conventional system for designated tangible cultural properties. This registration system was also extended to tangible [[folk cultural properties]] and [[:en:Memorials|Memorials]] (including [[historical sites]], [[places of scenic beauty]], and [[natural monuments]]). (Under the provisions of the Cultural Property Protection Law, the terms '''"designation"''' and '''"registration"''' are clearly distinguished, so it is incorrect to state on websites or tourist guides that a property is "designated" when it is in fact "registered.")
The Yokohama City Regional Cultural Property system, while similar to the national registration system, was actually established earlier, in 1988 (Shōwa 63). It is designed to complement the designated cultural property system by providing a more "relaxed form of protection"—one that does not impose the same level of legal binding force, relying instead on the understanding and cooperation of local residents.
Properties are registered following reviews held annually by the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kyoiku/shingikai/bunkazai/], which convene each fiscal year based on requests from local history enthusiasts and cultural property owners.

[[File:生麦事件碑 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|One example of a City Registered Regional Cultural Property: the Namamugi Incident Monument]]
== Overview == This system allows for the registration of historic [[:en:Cultural heritage|Cultural heritage]] within various areas of Yokohama City—items that have been preserved by local residents and are essential for understanding the region—in order to promote their protection and utilization. Properties registered under this system are also referred to as "Regional Cultural Properties" or "Yokohama City Regional Cultural Properties."
(See also the entry on '''[[Yokohama City Designated Cultural Properties and Regional Cultural Properties List]]'''.)
Following the 1996 (Heisei 8) amendment to the {{ill|Cultural Property Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural Property Protection Law|1=Act on Protection of Cultural Properties|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1188502}}, a registration system for cultural properties was established in addition to the conventional system for designated tangible cultural properties. This registration system was also extended to tangible [[folk cultural properties]] and [[:en:Memorials|Memorials]] (including [[historical sites]], [[places of scenic beauty]], and [[natural monuments]]). (Under the provisions of the Cultural Property Protection Law, the terms '''"designation"''' and '''"registration"''' are clearly distinguished, so it is incorrect to state on websites or tourist guides that a property is "designated" when it is in fact "registered.")
The Yokohama City Regional Cultural Property system, while similar to the national registration system, was actually established earlier, in 1988 (Shōwa 63). It is designed to complement the designated cultural property system by providing a more "relaxed form of protection"—one that does not impose the same level of legal binding force, relying instead on the understanding and cooperation of local residents.
Properties are registered following reviews held annually by the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kyoiku/shingikai/bunkazai/], which convene each fiscal year based on requests from local history enthusiasts and cultural property owners.

[[Category:Japanese cultural heritage protection system]] [[Category:History of Yokohama]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q30928910}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q30928910]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Referencesの明記|date=2018-02-11}} '''Yokohama City Registered Regional Cultural Property''' (横浜市登録地域文化財) is one of the protection systems for [[registered tangible cultural properties]] uniquely implemented by [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]] under the provisions of the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001174.html#e000000737] Chapter 6 (Articles 45–49).<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Board of Education|1=Yokohama City Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543020}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0027_20240105.pdf|accessdate=2024-10-22}}</ref>
[[File:生麦事件碑 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|One example of a City Registered Regional Cultural Property: the Namamugi Incident Monument]]
== Overview == This system allows for the registration of historic [[:en:Cultural heritage|Cultural heritage]] within various areas of Yokohama City—items that have been preserved by local residents and are essential for understanding the region—in order to promote their protection and utilization. Properties registered under this system are also referred to as "Regional Cultural Properties" or "Yokohama City Regional Cultural Properties."
(See also the entry on '''[[Yokohama City Designated Cultural Properties and Regional Cultural Properties List]]'''.)
Following the 1996 (Heisei 8) amendment to the {{ill|Cultural Property Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural Property Protection Law|1=Act on Protection of Cultural Properties|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1188502}}, a registration system for cultural properties was established in addition to the conventional system for designated tangible cultural properties. This registration system was also extended to tangible [[folk cultural properties]] and [[:en:Memorials|Memorials]] (including [[historical sites]], [[places of scenic beauty]], and [[natural monuments]]). (Under the provisions of the Cultural Property Protection Law, the terms '''"designation"''' and '''"registration"''' are clearly distinguished, so it is incorrect to state on websites or tourist guides that a property is "designated" when it is in fact "registered.")
The Yokohama City Regional Cultural Property system, while similar to the national registration system, was actually established earlier, in 1988 (Shōwa 63). It is designed to complement the designated cultural property system by providing a more "relaxed form of protection"—one that does not impose the same level of legal binding force, relying instead on the understanding and cooperation of local residents.
Properties are registered following reviews held annually by the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kyoiku/shingikai/bunkazai/], which convene each fiscal year based on requests from local history enthusiasts and cultural property owners.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q30928910]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Referencesの明記|date=2018-02-11}} '''Yokohama City Registered Regional Cultural Property''' (横浜市登録地域文化財) is one of the protection systems for [[registered tangible cultural properties]] uniquely implemented by [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]] under the provisions of the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001174.html#e000000737] Chapter 6 (Articles 45–49).<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Board of Education|1=Yokohama City Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543020}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0027_20240105.pdf|accessdate=2024-10-22}}</ref>
[[File:生麦事件碑 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|One example of a City Registered Regional Cultural Property: the Namamugi Incident Monument]]
== Overview == This system allows for the registration of historic [[:en:Cultural heritage|Cultural heritage]] within various areas of Yokohama City—items that have been preserved by local residents and are essential for understanding the region—in order to promote their protection and utilization. Properties registered under this system are also referred to as "Regional Cultural Properties" or "Yokohama City Regional Cultural Properties."
(See also the entry on '''[[Yokohama City Designated Cultural Properties and Regional Cultural Properties List]]'''.)
Following the 1996 (Heisei 8) amendment to the {{ill|Cultural Property Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural Property Protection Law|1=Act on Protection of Cultural Properties|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1188502}}, a registration system for cultural properties was established in addition to the conventional system for designated tangible cultural properties. This registration system was also extended to tangible [[folk cultural properties]] and [[:en:Memorials|Memorials]] (including [[historical sites]], [[places of scenic beauty]], and [[natural monuments]]). (Under the provisions of the Cultural Property Protection Law, the terms '''"designation"''' and '''"registration"''' are clearly distinguished, so it is incorrect to state on websites or tourist guides that a property is "designated" when it is in fact "registered.")
The Yokohama City Regional Cultural Property system, while similar to the national registration system, was actually established earlier, in 1988 (Shōwa 63). It is designed to complement the designated cultural property system by providing a more "relaxed form of protection"—one that does not impose the same level of legal binding force, relying instead on the understanding and cooperation of local residents.
Properties are registered following reviews held annually by the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kyoiku/shingikai/bunkazai/], which convene each fiscal year based on requests from local history enthusiasts and cultural property owners.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q30928910]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Referencesの明記|date=2018-02-11}} '''Yokohama City Registered Regional Cultural Property''' (横浜市登録地域文化財) is one of the protection systems for [[registered tangible cultural properties]] uniquely implemented by [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]] under the provisions of the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001174.html#e000000737] Chapter 6 (Articles 45–49).<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Board of Education|1=Yokohama City Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543020}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0027_20240105.pdf|accessdate=2024-10-22}}</ref>
[[File:生麦事件碑 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|One example of a City Registered Regional Cultural Property: the Namamugi Incident Monument]]
== Overview == This system allows for the registration of historic [[:en:Cultural heritage|Cultural heritage]] within various areas of Yokohama City—items that have been preserved by local residents and are essential for understanding the region—in order to promote their protection and utilization. Properties registered under this system are also referred to as "Regional Cultural Properties" or "Yokohama City Regional Cultural Properties."
(See also the entry on '''[[Yokohama City Designated Cultural Properties and Regional Cultural Properties List]]'''.)
Following the 1996 (Heisei 8) amendment to the {{ill|Cultural Property Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural Property Protection Law|1=Act on Protection of Cultural Properties|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1188502}}, a registration system for cultural properties was established in addition to the conventional system for designated tangible cultural properties. This registration system was also extended to tangible [[folk cultural properties]] and [[:en:Memorials|Memorials]] (including [[historical sites]], [[places of scenic beauty]], and [[natural monuments]]). (Under the provisions of the Cultural Property Protection Law, the terms '''"designation"''' and '''"registration"''' are clearly distinguished, so it is incorrect to state on websites or tourist guides that a property is "designated" when it is in fact "registered.")
The Yokohama City Regional Cultural Property system, while similar to the national registration system, was actually established earlier, in 1988 (Shōwa 63). It is designed to complement the designated cultural property system by providing a more "relaxed form of protection"—one that does not impose the same level of legal binding force, relying instead on the understanding and cooperation of local residents.
Properties are registered following reviews held annually by the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kyoiku/shingikai/bunkazai/], which convene each fiscal year based on requests from local history enthusiasts and cultural property owners.
== Footnotes ==
{{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * [[Cultural Property Protection Law]] * [[Registered Tangible Cultural Property]] * [[:en:Cultural Property|Cultural Property]] * [[Yokohama City Designated Cultural Properties and Regional Cultural Properties List]] * [[:en:Buried Cultural Properties|Buried Cultural Properties]] * [[Kawasaki City Regional Cultural Properties]] – A similar system in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[:en:Kawasaki City|Kawasaki City]]. * [[Chiba City Regional Cultural Properties]] – A similar system in [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]] [[:en:Chiba City|Chiba City]].

[[File:生麦事件碑 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|One example of a City Registered Regional Cultural Property: the Namamugi Incident Monument]]
== Overview == This system allows for the registration of historic [[:en:Cultural heritage|Cultural heritage]] within various areas of Yokohama City—items that have been preserved by local residents and are essential for understanding the region—in order to promote their protection and utilization. Properties registered under this system are also referred to as "Regional Cultural Properties" or "Yokohama City Regional Cultural Properties."
(See also the entry on '''[[Yokohama City Designated Cultural Properties and Regional Cultural Properties List]]'''.)
Following the 1996 (Heisei 8) amendment to the {{ill|Cultural Property Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural Property Protection Law|1=Act on Protection of Cultural Properties|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1188502}}, a registration system for cultural properties was established in addition to the conventional system for designated tangible cultural properties. This registration system was also extended to tangible [[folk cultural properties]] and [[:en:Memorials|Memorials]] (including [[historical sites]], [[places of scenic beauty]], and [[natural monuments]]). (Under the provisions of the Cultural Property Protection Law, the terms '''"designation"''' and '''"registration"''' are clearly distinguished, so it is incorrect to state on websites or tourist guides that a property is "designated" when it is in fact "registered.")
The Yokohama City Regional Cultural Property system, while similar to the national registration system, was actually established earlier, in 1988 (Shōwa 63). It is designed to complement the designated cultural property system by providing a more "relaxed form of protection"—one that does not impose the same level of legal binding force, relying instead on the understanding and cooperation of local residents.
Properties are registered following reviews held annually by the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kyoiku/shingikai/bunkazai/], which convene each fiscal year based on requests from local history enthusiasts and cultural property owners.

{{wikidata link|Q30928910}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:History of Yokohama]] [[Category:Japanese cultural heritage protection system]] [[Category:Qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q30928910]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Referencesの明記|date=2018-02-11}} '''Yokohama City Registered Regional Cultural Property''' (横浜市登録地域文化財) is one of the protection systems for [[registered tangible cultural properties]] uniquely implemented by [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]] under the provisions of the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001174.html#e000000737] Chapter 6 (Articles 45–49).<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Board of Education|1=Yokohama City Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543020}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0027_20240105.pdf|accessdate=2024-10-22}}</ref>
[[File:生麦事件碑 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|One example of a City Registered Regional Cultural Property: the Namamugi Incident Monument]]
== Overview == This system allows for the registration of historic [[:en:Cultural heritage|Cultural heritage]] within various areas of Yokohama City—items that have been preserved by local residents and are essential for understanding the region—in order to promote their protection and utilization. Properties registered under this system are also referred to as "Regional Cultural Properties" or "Yokohama City Regional Cultural Properties."
(See also the entry on '''[[Yokohama City Designated Cultural Properties and Regional Cultural Properties List]]'''.)
Following the 1996 (Heisei 8) amendment to the {{ill|Cultural Property Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural Property Protection Law|1=Act on Protection of Cultural Properties|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1188502}}, a registration system for cultural properties was established in addition to the conventional system for designated tangible cultural properties. This registration system was also extended to tangible [[folk cultural properties]] and [[:en:Memorials|Memorials]] (including [[historical sites]], [[places of scenic beauty]], and [[natural monuments]]). (Under the provisions of the Cultural Property Protection Law, the terms '''"designation"''' and '''"registration"''' are clearly distinguished, so it is incorrect to state on websites or tourist guides that a property is "designated" when it is in fact "registered.")
The Yokohama City Regional Cultural Property system, while similar to the national registration system, was actually established earlier, in 1988 (Shōwa 63). It is designed to complement the designated cultural property system by providing a more "relaxed form of protection"—one that does not impose the same level of legal binding force, relying instead on the understanding and cooperation of local residents.
Properties are registered following reviews held annually by the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kyoiku/shingikai/bunkazai/], which convene each fiscal year based on requests from local history enthusiasts and cultural property owners.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q30928910]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Referencesの明記|date=2018-02-11}} '''Yokohama City Registered Regional Cultural Property''' (横浜市登録地域文化財) is one of the protection systems for [[registered tangible cultural properties]] uniquely implemented by {{ill|Yokohama|en|Yokohama|WD=Q38283|lt=Yokohama City}} under the provisions of the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001174.html#e000000737] Chapter 6 (Articles 45–49).<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Board of Education|1=Yokohama City Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543020}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0027_20240105.pdf|accessdate=2024-10-22}}</ref>
[[File:生麦事件碑 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|One example of a City Registered Regional Cultural Property: the Namamugi Incident Monument]]
== Overview == This system allows for the registration of historic {{ill|Cultural heritage|en|Cultural heritage|WD=Q210272|lt=Cultural heritage}} within various areas of Yokohama City—items that have been preserved by local residents and are essential for understanding the region—in order to promote their protection and utilization. Properties registered under this system are also referred to as "Regional Cultural Properties" or "Yokohama City Regional Cultural Properties."
(See also the entry on '''[[Yokohama City Designated Cultural Properties and Regional Cultural Properties List]]'''.)
Following the 1996 (Heisei 8) amendment to the {{ill|Cultural Property Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural Property Protection Law|1=Act on Protection of Cultural Properties|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1188502}}, a registration system for cultural properties was established in addition to the conventional system for designated tangible cultural properties. This registration system was also extended to tangible [[folk cultural properties]] and [[:en:Memorials|Memorials]] (including [[historical sites]], [[places of scenic beauty]], and [[natural monuments]]). (Under the provisions of the Cultural Property Protection Law, the terms '''"designation"''' and '''"registration"''' are clearly distinguished, so it is incorrect to state on websites or tourist guides that a property is "designated" when it is in fact "registered.")
The Yokohama City Regional Cultural Property system, while similar to the national registration system, was actually established earlier, in 1988 (Shōwa 63). It is designed to complement the designated cultural property system by providing a more "relaxed form of protection"—one that does not impose the same level of legal binding force, relying instead on the understanding and cooperation of local residents.
Properties are registered following reviews held annually by the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kyoiku/shingikai/bunkazai/], which convene each fiscal year based on requests from local history enthusiasts and cultural property owners.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:横浜市登録地域文化財]]
{{Referencesの明記|date=2018-02-11}} '''Yokohama City Registered Regional Cultural Property''' (横浜市登録地域文化財) is one of the protection systems for [[registered tangible cultural properties]] uniquely implemented by {{ill|Yokohama|en|Yokohama|WD=Q38283|lt=Yokohama City}} under the provisions of the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001174.html#e000000737] Chapter 6 (Articles 45–49).<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Board of Education|1=Yokohama City Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543020}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0027_20240105.pdf|accessdate=2024-10-22}}</ref>
[[File:生麦事件碑 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|One example of a City Registered Regional Cultural Property: the Namamugi Incident Monument]]
== Overview == This system allows for the registration of historic {{ill|Cultural heritage|en|Cultural heritage|WD=Q210272|lt=Cultural heritage}} within various areas of Yokohama City—items that have been preserved by local residents and are essential for understanding the region—in order to promote their protection and utilization. Properties registered under this system are also referred to as "Regional Cultural Properties" or "Yokohama City Regional Cultural Properties."
(See also the entry on '''[[Yokohama City Designated Cultural Properties and Regional Cultural Properties List]]'''.)
Following the 1996 (Heisei 8) amendment to the {{ill|Cultural Property Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural Property Protection Law|1=Act on Protection of Cultural Properties|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1188502}}, a registration system for cultural properties was established in addition to the conventional system for designated tangible cultural properties. This registration system was also extended to tangible [[folk cultural properties]] and [[:en:Memorials|Memorials]] (including [[historical sites]], [[places of scenic beauty]], and [[natural monuments]]). (Under the provisions of the Cultural Property Protection Law, the terms '''"designation"''' and '''"registration"''' are clearly distinguished, so it is incorrect to state on websites or tourist guides that a property is "designated" when it is in fact "registered.")
The Yokohama City Regional Cultural Property system, while similar to the national registration system, was actually established earlier, in 1988 (Shōwa 63). It is designed to complement the designated cultural property system by providing a more "relaxed form of protection"—one that does not impose the same level of legal binding force, relying instead on the understanding and cooperation of local residents.
Properties are registered following reviews held annually by the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kyoiku/shingikai/bunkazai/], which convene each fiscal year based on requests from local history enthusiasts and cultural property owners.
== Footnotes ==
{{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * [[Cultural Property Protection Law]] * [[Registered Tangible Cultural Property]] * [[:en:Cultural Property|Cultural Property]] * [[Yokohama City Designated Cultural Properties and Regional Cultural Properties List]] * [[:en:Buried Cultural Properties|Buried Cultural Properties]] * [[Kawasaki City Regional Cultural Properties]] – A similar system in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture}} [[:en:Kawasaki City|Kawasaki City]]. * [[Chiba City Regional Cultural Properties]] – A similar system in {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}} [[:en:Chiba City|Chiba City]].
== External Links == * [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001174.html Yokohama City Cultural Property Protection Ordinance] * [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001175.html Yokohama City Cultural Property Protection Ordinance Enforcement Regulations] * [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kyoiku/shingikai/bunkazai/ Yokohama City Cultural Property Protection Council]

{{Referencesの明記|date=2018-02-11}} '''Yokohama City Registered Regional Cultural Property''' (横浜市登録地域文化財) is one of the protection systems for [[registered tangible cultural properties]] uniquely implemented by {{ill|Yokohama|en|Yokohama|WD=Q38283|lt=Yokohama City|1=Yokohama}} under the provisions of the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001174.html#e000000737] Chapter 6 (Articles 45–49).<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Board of Education|1=Q11543020|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543020}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0027_20240105.pdf|accessdate=2024-10-22}}</ref>
[[File:生麦事件碑 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|One example of a City Registered Regional Cultural Property: the Namamugi Incident Monument]]
== Overview == This system allows for the registration of historic {{ill|Cultural heritage|en|Cultural heritage|WD=Q210272|lt=Cultural heritage|1=Cultural heritage}} within various areas of Yokohama City—items that have been preserved by local residents and are essential for understanding the region—in order to promote their protection and utilization. Properties registered under this system are also referred to as "Regional Cultural Properties" or "Yokohama City Regional Cultural Properties."
(See also the entry on '''[[Yokohama City Designated Cultural Properties and Regional Cultural Properties List]]'''.)
Following the 1996 (Heisei 8) amendment to the {{ill|Cultural Property Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural Property Protection Law|1=Cultural Properties Protection Law|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1188502}}, a registration system for cultural properties was established in addition to the conventional system for designated tangible cultural properties. This registration system was also extended to tangible [[folk cultural properties]] and [[:en:Memorials|Memorials]] (including [[historical sites]], [[places of scenic beauty]], and [[natural monuments]]). (Under the provisions of the Cultural Property Protection Law, the terms '''"designation"''' and '''"registration"''' are clearly distinguished, so it is incorrect to state on websites or tourist guides that a property is "designated" when it is in fact "registered.")
The Yokohama City Regional Cultural Property system, while similar to the national registration system, was actually established earlier, in 1988 (Shōwa 63). It is designed to complement the designated cultural property system by providing a more "relaxed form of protection"—one that does not impose the same level of legal binding force, relying instead on the understanding and cooperation of local residents.
Properties are registered following reviews held annually by the [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kyoiku/shingikai/bunkazai/], which convene each fiscal year based on requests from local history enthusiasts and cultural property owners.
== Footnotes ==
{{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * [[Cultural Property Protection Law]] * [[Registered Tangible Cultural Property]] * [[:en:Cultural Property|Cultural Property]] * [[Yokohama City Designated Cultural Properties and Regional Cultural Properties List]] * [[:en:Buried Cultural Properties|Buried Cultural Properties]] * [[Kawasaki City Regional Cultural Properties]] – A similar system in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}} [[:en:Kawasaki City|Kawasaki City]]. * [[Chiba City Regional Cultural Properties]] – A similar system in {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}} [[:en:Chiba City|Chiba City]].
== External Links == * [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001174.html Yokohama City Cultural Property Protection Ordinance] * [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/ex/reiki/reiki_honbun/g202RG00001175.html Yokohama City Cultural Property Protection Ordinance Enforcement Regulations] * [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kyoiku/shingikai/bunkazai/ Yokohama City Cultural Property Protection Council]
